'''少弐 資能'''（しょうに すけよし）（[[1198年]]ー[[1281年]]）は[[鎌倉幕府]]の有力御家人。[[武藤資頼]]の子。
武藤氏は当時、[[大宰府]]の責任者として太宰少弐の地位にあったため、この資能の時から少弐姓を名乗るようになったという。
資能は北九州に大きな勢力を持ち、幕府の鎮西探題として働いていた。[[1268年]]、モンゴルの使者の対応にも当たっている。[[元寇]]直前に出家して覚恵と号して家督を長男の[[少弐経資]]に譲ったが、自身も対元の総司令官として

'''少弐 資能'''（しょうに すけよし、[[建久]]9年（[[1198年]]） - [[弘安]]4年（[[1281年]]））は[[鎌倉幕府]]の有力[[御家人]]。[[武藤資頼]]の子。
[[武藤氏]]は当時、[[大宰府]]の責任者として太宰少弐の地位にあったため、この資能の時から少弐姓を名乗るようになったという。
資能は北九州に大きな勢力を持ち、幕府の[[鎮西探題]]として働いていた。[[1268年]]、モンゴルの使者の対応にも当たっている。[[元寇]]直前に出家して覚恵と号して家督を長男の[[少弐経資]]に譲ったが、自身も対元の総司令官として参戦した。

'''少弐 資能'''（しょうに すけよし、[[建久]]9年（[[1198年]]） - [[弘安]]4年（[[1281年]]））は[[鎌倉幕府]]の有力[[御家人]]。[[武藤資頼]]の子。
[[武藤氏]]は当時、[[大宰府]]の責任者として太宰少弐の地位にあったため、この資能の時から少弐姓を名乗るようになったという。
資能は北九州に大きな勢力を持ち、幕府の[[鎮西探題]]として働いていた。[[1268年]]、モンゴルの使者の対応にも当たっている。[[元寇]]直前に出家して覚恵と号して家督を長男の[[少弐経資]]に譲ったが、老齢の身でありながら自身も対元の総司令官として参戦した。しかしこのとき、奮戦したものの[[博多]]の街を元軍によって焼かれたため、戦後に非難を浴びている。
[[文永の役]]にも老齢の身を押して出陣し、[[壱岐島]]の戦いで元軍相手に奮戦したが、このとき敵兵の攻撃を受けて重傷となり、まもなくその傷がもとで死去したという。

'''少弐資能'''（'''しょうに すけよし'''、[[建久]]9年（[[1198年]]） - [[弘安]]4年（[[1281年]]））は[[鎌倉幕府]]の有力[[御家人]]。[[武藤資頼]]の子。
[[武藤氏]]は当時、[[大宰府]]の責任者として太宰少弐の地位にあったため、この資能の時から少弐姓を名乗るようになったという。
資能は北九州に大きな勢力を持ち、幕府の[[鎮西探題]]として働いていた。[[1268年]]、蒙古の使者の対応にも当たっている。[[元寇]]直前に出家して覚恵と号して家督を長男の[[少弐経資]]に譲ったが、老齢の身でありながら自身も対元の総司令官として参戦した。しかしこのとき、奮戦したものの[[博多]]の街を元軍によって焼かれたため、戦後に非難を浴びている。
[[文永の役]]にも老齢の身を押して出陣し、[[壱岐島]]の戦いで元軍相手に奮戦したが、このとき敵兵の攻撃を受けて重傷となり、まもなくその傷がもとで死去したという。

'''少弐 資能'''（しょうに すけよし、[[建久]]9年（[[1198年]]） - [[弘安]]4年閏[[7月13日 (旧暦)|7月13日]]（[[1281年]][[8月28日]]））は[[鎌倉幕府]]の有力[[御家人]]。[[武藤資頼]]の子。'''武藤 資能'''とも。子に[[少弐経資]]、[[少弐景資]]。
当時[[武藤氏]]は[[大宰府]]の責任者として[[大宰少弐]]の地位にあったため、資能の代から[[少弐氏|少弐]]姓を名乗るようになったという。

[[弘安の役]]にも老齢の身を押して出陣した。[[壱岐島]]の戦いでは東路軍相手に奮戦し、壱岐島から東路軍を駆逐した。しかし、この時の戦闘で蒙古兵の攻撃を受けて重傷を受け、その傷がもとでまもなく死去したという。享年84。

当時[[武藤氏]]は[[大宰府]]の責任者として[[大宰府|大宰少弐]]の地位にあったため、資能の代から[[少弐氏|少弐]]姓を名乗るようになったという。

弘安4年（1281年）の[[元寇#弘安の役|弘安の役]]にも老齢の身を押して出陣した。[[元寇#壱岐島の戦い|壱岐島の戦い]]では元軍を相手に奮戦し、壱岐島から元軍を駆逐した<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref>。しかし、この時の負傷がもとでまもなく死去したという。享年84。

revision.comment: 『武藤少弐興亡史』の記述を加筆
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 武士 | 氏名 = 少弐資能 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[鎌倉時代]]前期 - 中期 | 生誕 = [[建久]]9年（[[1198年]]） | 死没 = [[弘安]]4年閏[[7月13日 (旧暦)|7月13日]]（[[1281年]][[8月28日]]） | 改名 = 資能→覚恵（法号） | 別名 = 武藤資能 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 霊名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[大宰府|大宰少弐]] | 幕府 = [[鎌倉幕府]] | 主君 = [[藤原頼経]]→[[藤原頼嗣|頼嗣]]→[[宗尊親王]]→[[惟康親王]] | 藩 = | 氏族 = [[少弐氏]] | 父母 = 父：[[武藤資頼]] | 兄弟 = '''資能'''、[[武藤頼茂]]、[[武藤為頼]]、[[武藤頼基]] | 妻 = | 子 = [[少弐経資|経資]]、[[少弐景資|景資]]、[[少弐盛資|盛資]]<ref name="sonpibunmyaku">『尊卑分脈』第2篇・391頁。</ref>、[[少弐宗資|宗資]]? | 特記事項 = }}
'''少弐 資能'''（しょうに すけよし）は、[[鎌倉時代]]前期から中期にかけての[[武将]]・[[御家人]]。[[少弐氏]]2代当主。
== 略歴 == 当時[[武藤氏]]は[[大宰府]]の責任者として[[大宰府|大宰少弐]]の地位にあったため、資能の代から[[少弐氏|少弐]]姓を名乗るようになったという。
資能は北九州に大きな勢力を持ち、幕府の[[鎮西奉行]]として、[[文永]]5年（[[1268年]]）、[[モンゴル|蒙古]]の使者の対応にも当たっている<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref>。[[元寇#文永の役|文永の役]]直前に出家して覚恵と号し家督を長男・[[少弐経資|経資]]に譲ったが、老齢の身でありながら自身も対[[元 (王朝)|元]]の総司令官として参戦し、元軍の侵攻を阻止し撃退することに成功する。
弘安4年（1281年）の[[元寇#弘安の役|弘安の役]]にも老齢の身を押して出陣した。[[元寇#壱岐島の戦い|壱岐島の戦い]]では元軍を相手に奮戦し、壱岐島から元軍を駆逐した<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref>。しかし、この時の負傷がもとでまもなく死去したという。享年84。大応国師[[南浦紹明]]<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年(p.68)</ref>は、導師として[[太宰府]]横岳[[崇福寺]]において葬儀を執り行った。
なお、仁治3年（1242年）に、[[円爾]]（[[聖一国師]]）が[[博多]]に[[承天寺]]<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年(p.12)の解釈によると、父の武藤資頼の生前の約束であったのか、没後14年を経ているにもかかわらず、承天寺の喜捨(捨地壇越)は父資頼として、その名をたたえているのである。</ref> を開く際に、資能は寺地数万坪を寄進した。
[[太宰府市]]の[[観世音寺]]の北にある観世音寺四十九子院跡<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html に九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>のひとつといわれる安養寺の跡地の一角には、[[武藤資頼]]・資能の墓と伝えられる五輪塔と宝篋印塔が並んで建っています。

{{基礎情報 武士 | 氏名 = 少弐資能 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[鎌倉時代]]前期 - 中期 | 生誕 = [[建久]]9年（[[1198年]]） | 死没 = [[弘安]]4年閏[[7月13日 (旧暦)|7月13日]]（[[1281年]][[8月28日]]） | 改名 = 資能→覚恵（法号） | 別名 = 武藤資能 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 霊名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[大宰府|大宰少弐]] | 幕府 = [[鎌倉幕府]] | 主君 = [[藤原頼経]]→[[藤原頼嗣|頼嗣]]→[[宗尊親王]]→[[惟康親王]] | 藩 = | 氏族 = [[少弐氏]] | 父母 = 父：[[武藤資頼]] | 兄弟 = '''資能'''、[[武藤頼茂]]、[[武藤為頼]]、[[武藤頼基]] | 妻 = | 子 = [[少弐経資|経資]]、[[少弐景資|景資]]、[[少弐盛資|盛資]]<ref name="sonpibunmyaku">『尊卑分脈』第2篇・391頁。</ref>、[[少弐宗資|宗資]]? | 特記事項 = }}
'''少弐 資能'''（しょうに すけよし）は、[[鎌倉時代]]前期から中期にかけての[[武将]]・[[御家人]]。[[少弐氏]]2代当主。
== 略歴 == 当時[[武藤氏]]は[[大宰府]]の責任者として[[大宰府|大宰少弐]]の地位にあったため、資能の代から[[少弐氏|少弐]]姓を名乗るようになったという。
資能は北九州に大きな勢力を持ち、幕府の[[鎮西奉行]]として、[[文永]]5年（[[1268年]]）、[[モンゴル|蒙古]]の使者の対応にも当たっている<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref>。[[元寇#文永の役|文永の役]]直前に出家して覚恵と号し家督を長男・[[少弐経資|経資]]に譲ったが、老齢の身でありながら自身も対[[元 (王朝)|元]]の総司令官として参戦し、元軍の侵攻を阻止し撃退することに成功する。
弘安4年（1281年）の[[元寇#弘安の役|弘安の役]]にも老齢の身を押して出陣した。[[元寇#壱岐島の戦い|壱岐島の戦い]]では元軍を相手に奮戦し、壱岐島から元軍を駆逐した<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref>。しかし、この時の負傷がもとでまもなく死去したという。享年84。大応国師[[南浦紹明]]<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年(p.68)</ref>は、導師として[[太宰府]]横岳[[崇福寺]]において葬儀を執り行った。
なお、仁治3年（1242年）に、[[円爾]]（[[聖一国師]]）が[[博多]]に[[承天寺]]<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年(p.12)の解釈によると、父の武藤資頼の生前の約束であったのか、没後14年を経ているにもかかわらず、承天寺の喜捨(捨地壇越)は父資頼として、その名をたたえているのである。</ref> を開く際に、資能は寺地数万坪を寄進した。
[[太宰府市]]の[[観世音寺]]の北にある観世音寺四十九子院跡<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html に九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>のひとつといわれる安養寺の跡地の一角には、[[武藤資頼]]墓と伝えられる五輪塔と少弐資能の供養塔(宝篋印塔)が並んで建っています。
== 脚注 == <references/>
== 出典 == *『[[尊卑分脈]]』 *『亀山天皇紀』 *『後宇多天皇紀』 *渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年
== 登場作品 == *[[国難 (映画)|国難]]（1920年、映画） - 演：[[児玉英一]] *[[日蓮と蒙古大襲来]]（1958年、映画） - 演：[[杉山昌三九]] *[[北条時宗 (NHK大河ドラマ)|北条時宗]]（2001年、NHK大河ドラマ） - 演：[[野口貴史]] *[[アンゴルモア 元寇合戦記]](2013年、漫画)

{{基礎情報 武士 | 氏名 = 少弐資能 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[鎌倉時代]]前期 - 中期 | 生誕 = [[建久]]9年（[[1198年]]） | 死没 = [[弘安]]4年閏[[7月13日 (旧暦)|7月13日]]（[[1281年]][[8月28日]]） | 改名 = 資能→覚恵（法号） | 別名 = 武藤資能 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 霊名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[大宰府|大宰少弐]] | 幕府 = [[鎌倉幕府]] | 主君 = [[藤原頼経]]→[[藤原頼嗣|頼嗣]]→[[宗尊親王]]→[[惟康親王]] | 藩 = | 氏族 = [[少弐氏]] | 父母 = 父：[[武藤資頼]] | 兄弟 = '''資能'''、[[武藤頼茂]]、[[武藤為頼]]、[[武藤頼基]] | 妻 = | 子 = [[少弐経資|経資]]、[[少弐景資|景資]]、[[少弐盛資|盛資]]<ref name="sonpibunmyaku">『尊卑分脈』第2篇・391頁。</ref>、[[少弐宗資|宗資]]? | 特記事項 = }}
'''少弐 資能'''（しょうに すけよし）は、[[鎌倉時代]]前期から中期にかけての[[武将]]・[[御家人]]。[[少弐氏]]2代当主。
== 略歴 == 当時[[武藤氏]]は[[大宰府]]の責任者として[[大宰府|大宰少弐]]の地位にあったため、資能の代から[[少弐氏|少弐]]姓を名乗るようになったという。
資能は北九州に大きな勢力を持ち、幕府の[[鎮西奉行]]として、[[文永]]5年（[[1268年]]）、[[モンゴル|蒙古]]の使者の対応にも当たっている<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref>。[[元寇#文永の役|文永の役]]直前に出家して覚恵と号し家督を長男の[[少弐経資|経資]]に譲ったが、老齢の身でありながら自身も対[[元 (王朝)|元]]の総司令官として参戦し、元軍の侵攻を阻止し撃退することに成功する。
[[弘安]]4年（[[1281年]]）の[[元寇#弘安の役|弘安の役]]にも老齢の身を押して出陣した。[[元寇#壱岐島の戦い|壱岐島の戦い]]では元軍を相手に奮戦し、壱岐島から元軍を駆逐した<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref>。しかし、この時の負傷がもとでまもなく死去したという。享年84。大応国師[[南浦紹明]]<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年(p.68)</ref>は、導師として[[太宰府]]横岳[[崇福寺]]において葬儀を執り行った。
なお、[[仁治]]3年（[[1242年]]）に、[[円爾]]（聖一国師）が[[博多]]に[[承天寺]]<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年(p.12)の解釈によると、父の武藤資頼の生前の約束であったのか、没後14年を経ているにもかかわらず、承天寺の喜捨(捨地壇越)は父資頼として、その名をたたえているのである。</ref> を開く際に、資能は寺地数万坪を寄進した。
[[太宰府市]]の[[観世音寺]]の北にある観世音寺四十九子院跡<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html に九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>のひとつといわれる安養寺の跡地の一角には、[[武藤資頼]]墓と伝えられる五輪塔と少弐資能の供養塔(宝篋印塔)が並んで建っている。
== 脚注 == <references />
== 出典 == *『[[尊卑分脈]]』 *『亀山天皇紀』 *『後宇多天皇紀』 *渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年
{{少弐氏当主}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しように すけよし}} [[Category:武藤氏|すけよし]] [[Category:少弐氏|すけよし]] [[Category:鎌倉幕府御家人]] [[Category:元寇の人物]] [[Category:1198年生]] [[Category:1281年没]]

== 略歴 == 当時[[武藤氏]]は[[大宰府]]の責任者として[[大宰府|大宰少弐]]の地位にあったため、資能の代から[[少弐氏|少弐]]姓を名乗るようになったという。
資能は北九州に大きな勢力を持ち、幕府の[[鎮西奉行]]として、[[文永]]5年（[[1268年]]）、[[元 (王朝)|元]]の使者の対応にも当たっている<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref>。[[元寇|文永の役]]直前に出家して覚恵と号し家督を長男の[[少弐経資|経資]]に譲ったが、老齢の身でありながら自身も九州の総司令官として参戦し、元軍の侵攻を阻止し撃退することに成功する。
[[弘安]]4年（[[1281年]]）の[[元寇|弘安の役]]にも老齢の身を押して出陣した。[[元寇|壱岐島の戦い]]では元軍を相手に奮戦し、壱岐島から元軍を駆逐した<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref>。しかし、この時の負傷がもとでまもなく死去したという。享年84。大応国師[[南浦紹明]]<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年(p.68)</ref>は、導師として[[太宰府]]横岳[[崇福寺 (福岡市)|崇福寺]]において葬儀を執り行った。
なお、[[仁治]]3年（[[1242年]]）に、[[円爾]]（聖一国師）が[[博多]]に[[承天寺]]<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年(p.12)の解釈によると、父の武藤資頼の生前の約束であったのか、没後14年を経ているにもかかわらず、承天寺の喜捨(捨地壇越)は父資頼として、その名をたたえているのである。</ref> を開く際に、資能は寺地数万坪を寄進した。
[[太宰府市]]の[[観世音寺]]の北にある観世音寺四十九子院跡<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html に九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>のひとつといわれる安養寺の跡地の一角には、[[武藤資頼]]墓と伝えられる五輪塔と少弐資能の供養塔(宝篋印塔)が並んで建っている。
== 脚注 == <references />
== 出典 == *『[[尊卑分脈]]』 *『亀山天皇紀』 *『後宇多天皇紀』 *渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年
{{少弐氏当主}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しように すけよし}} [[Category:武藤氏|すけよし]] [[Category:少弐氏|すけよし]] [[Category:鎌倉幕府御家人]] [[Category:元寇の人物]] [[Category:13世紀アジアの軍人]] [[Category:13世紀日本の人物]] [[Category:1198年生]] [[Category:1281年没]]

'''少弐 資能'''（しょうに すけよし）は、[[鎌倉時代]]前期から中期にかけての[[武将]]・[[御家人]]。[[少弐氏]]2代当主。
== 略歴 == 当時[[武藤氏]]は[[大宰府]]の責任者として[[大宰府|大宰少弐]]の地位にあったため、資能の代から[[少弐氏|少弐]]姓を名乗るようになったという。
資能は北九州に大きな勢力を持ち、幕府の[[鎮西奉行]]として、[[文永]]5年（[[1268年]]）、[[元 (王朝)|元]]の使者の対応にも当たっている<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref>。[[元寇|文永の役]]直前に出家して覚恵と号し家督を長男の[[少弐経資|経資]]に譲ったが、老齢の身でありながら自身も九州の総司令官として参戦し、元軍の侵攻を阻止し撃退することに成功する。
[[弘安]]4年（[[1281年]]）の[[元寇|弘安の役]]にも老齢の身を押して出陣した。[[元寇|壱岐島の戦い]]では元軍を相手に奮戦し、壱岐島から元軍を駆逐した<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref>。しかし、この時の負傷がもとでまもなく死去したという。享年84。大応国師[[南浦紹明]]<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年(p.68)</ref>は、導師として[[太宰府]]横岳[[崇福寺 (福岡市)|崇福寺]]において葬儀を執り行った。
なお、[[仁治]]3年（[[1242年]]）に、[[円爾]]（聖一国師）が[[博多]]に[[承天寺]]<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年(p.12)の解釈によると、父の武藤資頼の生前の約束であったのか、没後14年を経ているにもかかわらず、承天寺の喜捨(捨地壇越)は父資頼として、その名をたたえているのである。</ref> を開く際に、資能は寺地数万坪を寄進した。

{{ill|Shōni Sukenaga|ja|少弐資能}}
[[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[Category:Https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[Category:Https://ko.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80%EC%9A%94%EC%8B%9C]] [[zh:%E5%B0%91%E8%B4%B0%E8%B5%84%E8%83%BD]] [[ja:%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E8%B3%87%E8%83%BD]] {{Infobox samurai | name = Shōni Sukeyoshi (少弐 資能) | image = | imagesize = | caption = | era = Early to mid-[[Kamakura period]] | birth_date = [[Kenkyū]] 9 (1198) | death_date = [[Kōan]] 4, intercalary [[7th month]], [[13th day (lunar calendar)|13th day]] (August 28, 1281) | posthumous_name = Kakue (覚恵; Dharma name) | alias = Muto Sukeyoshi | rank = [[Dazai Shōni|Assistant Governor of Dazaifu]] | allegiance = [[Kamakura shogunate]] | lord = [[Fujiwara no Yoritsune|Yoritsune]], [[Fujiwara no Yoritsugu|Yoritsugu]], [[Prince Munetaka]], [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}} | siblings = Sukeyoshi, {{ill|Muto Yorishige|ja|武藤頼茂}}, {{ill|Muto Tameyori|ja|武藤為頼}}, {{ill|Muto Yorimoto|ja|武藤頼基}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資}}, {{ill|Shōni Kagesue|ja|少弐景資}}, {{ill|Shōni Morisue|ja|少弐盛資}}<ref name="sonpibunmyaku">『尊卑分脈』第2篇・391頁。</ref>, {{ill|Shōni Munetsune|ja|少弐宗資}}? | notable = }}
'''Shōni Sukeyoshi''' (少弐 資能) was a [[samurai]] and retainer of the [[Kamakura shogunate]] active during the early to mid-[[Kamakura period]]. He was the second head of the {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏}}.
== Biography ==
During Sukeyoshi’s time, the {{ill|Muto clan|ja|武藤氏}} held the position of [[Dazai Shōni]], a role associated with governance in the {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|大宰府}} region. This responsibility led to the family adopting the surname {{ill|Shōni|ja|少弐氏}} starting from Sukeyoshi’s generation.
Sukeyoshi wielded significant power in northern Kyushu and served as the shogunate's {{ill|Chinzei Bugyō|ja|鎮西奉行}}. In [[Bun'ei]] 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the [[Yuan dynasty]].<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}} (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資}}. Despite his age, Sukeyoshi personally led the Kyushu defense forces and successfully repelled the invading Yuan forces.
In [[Kōan]] 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the {{ill|Second Mongol invasion of Japan|ja|弘安の役}}. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明}} at {{ill|Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)|ja|崇福寺}}, {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府}}.
In [[Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the {{ill|Shōten-ji|ja|承天寺}} in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年(p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡}} in {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府市}}, the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a {{ill|Hōkyōintō|ja|宝篋印塔}} memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>
== References == <references />
== Sources == * 『尊卑分脈』 * 『亀山天皇紀』 * 『後宇多天皇紀』 * 渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年

Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡}} in {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府市}}, the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a {{ill|Hōkyōintō|ja|宝篋印塔}} memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>

{{ill|Shōni Sukenaga|ja|少弐資能}}
[[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[Category:Https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[Category:Https://ko.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80%EC%9A%94%EC%8B%9C]] [[zh:%E5%B0%91%E8%B4%B0%E8%B5%84%E8%83%BD]] [[ja:%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E8%B3%87%E8%83%BD]] {{Infobox samurai | name = Shōni Sukeyoshi (少弐 資能) | image = | imagesize = | caption = | era = Early to mid-[[Kamakura period]] | birth_date = [[Kenkyū]] 9 (1198) | death_date = [[Kōan]] 4, intercalary [[7th month]], [[13th day (lunar calendar)|13th day]] (August 28, 1281) | posthumous_name = Kakue (覚恵; Dharma name) | alias = Muto Sukeyoshi | rank = [[Dazai Shōni|Assistant Governor of Dazaifu]] | allegiance = [[Kamakura shogunate]] | lord = [[Fujiwara no Yoritsune|Yoritsune]], [[Fujiwara no Yoritsugu|Yoritsugu]], [[Prince Munetaka]], [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}} | siblings = Sukeyoshi, {{ill|Muto Yorishige|ja|武藤頼茂}}, {{ill|Muto Tameyori|ja|武藤為頼}}, {{ill|Muto Yorimoto|ja|武藤頼基}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資}}, {{ill|Shōni Kagesue|ja|少弐景資}}, {{ill|Shōni Morisue|ja|少弐盛資}}<ref name="sonpibunmyaku">『尊卑分脈』第2篇・391頁。</ref>, {{ill|Shōni Munetsune|ja|少弐宗資}}? | notable = }}
'''Shōni Sukeyoshi''' (少弐 資能) was a [[samurai]] and retainer of the [[Kamakura shogunate]] active during the early to mid-[[Kamakura period]]. He was the second head of the {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏}}.
== Biography ==
During Sukeyoshi’s time, the {{ill|Muto clan|ja|武藤氏}} held the position of [[Dazai Shōni]], a role associated with governance in the {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|大宰府}} region. This responsibility led to the family adopting the surname {{ill|Shōni|ja|少弐氏}} starting from Sukeyoshi’s generation.
Sukeyoshi wielded significant power in northern Kyushu and served as the shogunate's {{ill|Chinzei Bugyō|ja|鎮西奉行}}. In [[Bun'ei]] 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the [[Yuan dynasty]].<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}} (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資}}. Despite his age, Sukeyoshi personally led the Kyushu defense forces and successfully repelled the invading Yuan forces.
In [[Kōan]] 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the {{ill|Second Mongol invasion of Japan|ja|弘安の役}}. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明}} at {{ill|Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)|ja|崇福寺}}, {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府}}.
In [[Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the {{ill|Shōten-ji|ja|承天寺}} in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年(p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡}} in {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府市}}, the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a {{ill|Hōkyōintō|ja|宝篋印塔}} memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>

'''Shōni Sukeyoshi''' (少弐 資能) was a [[samurai]] and retainer of the [[Kamakura shogunate]] active during the early to mid-[[Kamakura period]]. He was the second head of the {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏}}.
== Biography ==
During Sukeyoshi’s time, the {{ill|Muto clan|ja|武藤氏}} held the position of [[Dazai Shōni]], a role associated with governance in the {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|大宰府}} region. This responsibility led to the family adopting the surname {{ill|Shōni|ja|少弐氏}} starting from Sukeyoshi’s generation.
Sukeyoshi wielded significant power in northern Kyushu and served as the shogunate's {{ill|Chinzei Bugyō|ja|鎮西奉行}}. In [[Bun'ei]] 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the [[Yuan dynasty]].<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}} (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資}}. Despite his age, Sukeyoshi personally led the Kyushu defense forces and successfully repelled the invading Yuan forces.
In [[Kōan]] 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the {{ill|Second Mongol invasion of Japan|ja|弘安の役}}. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明}} at {{ill|Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)|ja|崇福寺}}, {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府}}.
In [[Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the {{ill|Shōten-ji|ja|承天寺}} in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年(p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡}} in {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府市}}, the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a {{ill|Hōkyōintō|ja|宝篋印塔}} memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>

He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明}} at {{ill|Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)|ja|崇福寺}}, {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府}}.
In [[Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the {{ill|Shōten-ji|ja|承天寺}} in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年(p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡}} in {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府市}}, the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a {{ill|Hōkyōintō|ja|宝篋印塔}} memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>

'''Shōni Sukeyoshi''' (少弐 資能) was a [[samurai]] and retainer of the [[Kamakura shogunate]] active during the early to mid-[[Kamakura period]]. He was the second head of the {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏}}.
== Biography ==
During Sukeyoshi’s time, the {{ill|Muto clan|ja|武藤氏}} held the position of [[Dazai Shōni]], a role associated with governance in the {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|大宰府}} region. This responsibility led to the family adopting the surname {{ill|Shōni|ja|少弐氏}} starting from Sukeyoshi’s generation.
Sukeyoshi wielded significant power in northern Kyushu and served as the shogunate's {{ill|Chinzei Bugyō|ja|鎮西奉行}}. In [[Bun'ei]] 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the [[Yuan dynasty]].<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}} (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資}}. Despite his age, Sukeyoshi personally led the Kyushu defense forces and successfully repelled the invading Yuan forces.
In [[Kōan]] 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the {{ill|Second Mongol invasion of Japan|ja|弘安の役}}. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明}} at {{ill|Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)|ja|崇福寺}}, {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府}}.
In [[Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the {{ill|Shōten-ji|ja|承天寺}} in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年(p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡}} in {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府市}}, the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a {{ill|Hōkyōintō|ja|宝篋印塔}} memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>

[[Category:Https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[Category:Https://ko.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80%EC%9A%94%EC%8B%9C]] [[zh:%E5%B0%91%E8%B4%B0%E8%B5%84%E8%83%BD]] [[ja:%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E8%B3%87%E8%83%BD]] {{Infobox samurai | name = Shōni Sukeyoshi (少弐 資能) | image = | imagesize = | caption = | era = Early to mid-[[Kamakura period]] | birth_date = [[Kenkyū]] 9 (1198) | death_date = [[Kōan]] 4, intercalary [[7th month]], [[13th day (lunar calendar)|13th day]] (August 28, 1281) | posthumous_name = Kakue (覚恵; Dharma name) | alias = Muto Sukeyoshi | rank = [[Dazai Shōni|Assistant Governor of Dazaifu]] | allegiance = [[Kamakura shogunate]] | lord = [[Fujiwara no Yoritsune|Yoritsune]], [[Fujiwara no Yoritsugu|Yoritsugu]], [[Prince Munetaka]], [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}} | siblings = Sukeyoshi, {{ill|Muto Yorishige|ja|武藤頼茂}}, {{ill|Muto Tameyori|ja|武藤為頼}}, {{ill|Muto Yorimoto|ja|武藤頼基}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資}}, {{ill|Shōni Kagesue|ja|少弐景資}}, {{ill|Shōni Morisue|ja|少弐盛資}}<ref name="sonpibunmyaku">『尊卑分脈』第2篇・391頁。</ref>, {{ill|Shōni Munetsune|ja|少弐宗資}}? | notable = }}
'''Shōni Sukeyoshi''' (少弐 資能) was a [[samurai]] and retainer of the [[Kamakura shogunate]] active during the early to mid-[[Kamakura period]]. He was the second head of the {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏}}.
== Biography ==
During Sukeyoshi’s time, the {{ill|Muto clan|ja|武藤氏}} held the position of [[Dazai Shōni]], a role associated with governance in the {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|大宰府}} region. This responsibility led to the family adopting the surname {{ill|Shōni|ja|少弐氏}} starting from Sukeyoshi’s generation.
Sukeyoshi wielded significant power in northern Kyushu and served as the shogunate's {{ill|Chinzei Bugyō|ja|鎮西奉行}}. In [[Bun'ei]] 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the [[Yuan dynasty]].<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}} (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資}}. Despite his age, Sukeyoshi personally led the Kyushu defense forces and successfully repelled the invading Yuan forces.
In [[Kōan]] 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the {{ill|Second Mongol invasion of Japan|ja|弘安の役}}. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明}} at {{ill|Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)|ja|崇福寺}}, {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府}}.
In [[Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the {{ill|Shōten-ji|ja|承天寺}} in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年(p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡}} in {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府市}}, the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a {{ill|Hōkyōintō|ja|宝篋印塔}} memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>

In [[Bun'ei]] 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the [[Yuan dynasty]].<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}} (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資}}. Despite his age, Sukeyoshi personally led the Kyushu defense forces and successfully repelled the invading Yuan forces.
In [[Kōan]] 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the {{ill|Second Mongol invasion of Japan|ja|弘安の役}}. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明}} at {{ill|Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)|ja|崇福寺}}, {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府}}.
In [[Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the {{ill|Shōten-ji|ja|承天寺}} in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年(p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡}} in {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府市}}, the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a {{ill|Hōkyōintō|ja|宝篋印塔}} memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>

'''Shōni Sukeyoshi''' (少弐 資能) was a [[samurai]] and retainer of the [[Kamakura shogunate]] active during the early to mid-[[Kamakura period]]. He was the second head of the {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏}}.
== Biography ==
During Sukeyoshi’s time, the {{ill|Muto clan|ja|武藤氏}} held the position of [[Dazai Shōni]], a role associated with governance in the {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|大宰府}} region. This responsibility led to the family adopting the surname {{ill|Shōni|ja|少弐氏}} starting from Sukeyoshi’s generation.
Sukeyoshi wielded significant power in northern Kyushu and served as the shogunate's {{ill|Chinzei Bugyō|ja|鎮西奉行}}. In [[Bun'ei]] 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the [[Yuan dynasty]].<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}} (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資}}. Despite his age, Sukeyoshi personally led the Kyushu defense forces and successfully repelled the invading Yuan forces.
In [[Kōan]] 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the {{ill|Second Mongol invasion of Japan|ja|弘安の役}}. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明}} at {{ill|Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)|ja|崇福寺}}, {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府}}.
In [[Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the {{ill|Shōten-ji|ja|承天寺}} in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年(p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡}} in {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府市}}, the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a {{ill|Hōkyōintō|ja|宝篋印塔}} memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>

Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡}} in {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府市}}, the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a {{ill|Hōkyōintō|ja|宝篋印塔}} memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>

{{ill|Shōni Sukenaga|ja|少弐資能}}
[[Category:Https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[Category:Https://ko.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80%EC%9A%94%EC%8B%9C]] [[zh:%E5%B0%91%E8%B4%B0%E8%B5%84%E8%83%BD]] [[ja:%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E8%B3%87%E8%83%BD]] {{Infobox samurai | name = Shōni Sukeyoshi (少弐 資能) | image = | imagesize = | caption = | era = Early to mid-[[Kamakura period]] | birth_date = [[Kenkyū]] 9 (1198) | death_date = [[Kōan]] 4, intercalary [[7th month]], [[13th day (lunar calendar)|13th day]] (August 28, 1281) | posthumous_name = Kakue (覚恵; Dharma name) | alias = Muto Sukeyoshi | rank = [[Dazai Shōni|Assistant Governor of Dazaifu]] | allegiance = [[Kamakura shogunate]] | lord = [[Fujiwara no Yoritsune|Yoritsune]], [[Fujiwara no Yoritsugu|Yoritsugu]], [[Prince Munetaka]], [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}} | siblings = Sukeyoshi, {{ill|Muto Yorishige|ja|武藤頼茂}}, {{ill|Muto Tameyori|ja|武藤為頼}}, {{ill|Muto Yorimoto|ja|武藤頼基}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資}}, {{ill|Shōni Kagesue|ja|少弐景資}}, {{ill|Shōni Morisue|ja|少弐盛資}}<ref name="sonpibunmyaku">『尊卑分脈』第2篇・391頁。</ref>, {{ill|Shōni Munetsune|ja|少弐宗資}}? | notable = }}
'''Shōni Sukeyoshi''' (少弐 資能) was a [[samurai]] and retainer of the [[Kamakura shogunate]] active during the early to mid-[[Kamakura period]]. He was the second head of the {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏}}.
== Biography ==
During Sukeyoshi’s time, the {{ill|Muto clan|ja|武藤氏}} held the position of [[Dazai Shōni]], a role associated with governance in the {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|大宰府}} region. This responsibility led to the family adopting the surname {{ill|Shōni|ja|少弐氏}} starting from Sukeyoshi’s generation.
Sukeyoshi wielded significant power in northern Kyushu and served as the shogunate's {{ill|Chinzei Bugyō|ja|鎮西奉行}}. In [[Bun'ei]] 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the [[Yuan dynasty]].<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}} (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資}}. Despite his age, Sukeyoshi personally led the Kyushu defense forces and successfully repelled the invading Yuan forces.
In [[Kōan]] 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the {{ill|Second Mongol invasion of Japan|ja|弘安の役}}. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明}} at {{ill|Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)|ja|崇福寺}}, {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府}}.
In [[Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the {{ill|Shōten-ji|ja|承天寺}} in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年(p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡}} in {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府市}}, the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a {{ill|Hōkyōintō|ja|宝篋印塔}} memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>

{{ill|Shōni Sukenaga|ja|少弐資能}}
[[Category:Https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[Category:Https://ko.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80%EC%9A%94%EC%8B%9C]] [[zh:%E5%B0%91%E8%B4%B0%E8%B5%84%E8%83%BD]] [[ja:%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E8%B3%87%E8%83%BD]] {{Infobox samurai | name = Shōni Sukeyoshi (少弐 資能) | image = | imagesize = | caption = | era = Early to mid-[[Kamakura period]] | birth_date = [[Kenkyū]] 9 (1198) | death_date = [[Kōan]] 4, intercalary [[7th month]], [[13th day (lunar calendar)|13th day]] (August 28, 1281) | posthumous_name = Kakue (覚恵; Dharma name) | alias = Muto Sukeyoshi | rank = [[Dazai Shōni|Assistant Governor of Dazaifu]] | allegiance = [[Kamakura shogunate]] | lord = [[Fujiwara no Yoritsune|Yoritsune]], [[Fujiwara no Yoritsugu|Yoritsugu]], [[Prince Munetaka]], [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}} | siblings = Sukeyoshi, {{ill|Muto Yorishige|ja|武藤頼茂}}, {{ill|Muto Tameyori|ja|武藤為頼}}, {{ill|Muto Yorimoto|ja|武藤頼基}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資}}, {{ill|Shōni Kagesue|ja|少弐景資}}, {{ill|Shōni Morisue|ja|少弐盛資}}<ref name="sonpibunmyaku">『尊卑分脈』第2篇・391頁。</ref>, {{ill|Shōni Munetsune|ja|少弐宗資}}? | notable = }}
'''Shōni Sukeyoshi''' (少弐 資能) was a [[samurai]] and retainer of the [[Kamakura shogunate]] active during the early to mid-[[Kamakura period]]. He was the second head of the {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏}}.
== Biography ==
During Sukeyoshi’s time, the {{ill|Muto clan|ja|武藤氏}} held the position of [[Dazai Shōni]], a role associated with governance in the {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|大宰府}} region. This responsibility led to the family adopting the surname {{ill|Shōni|ja|少弐氏}} starting from Sukeyoshi’s generation.
Sukeyoshi wielded significant power in northern Kyushu and served as the shogunate's {{ill|Chinzei Bugyō|ja|鎮西奉行}}. In [[Bun'ei]] 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the [[Yuan dynasty]].<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}} (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資}}. Despite his age, Sukeyoshi personally led the Kyushu defense forces and successfully repelled the invading Yuan forces.
In [[Kōan]] 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the {{ill|Second Mongol invasion of Japan|ja|弘安の役}}. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明}} at {{ill|Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)|ja|崇福寺}}, {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府}}.
In [[Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the {{ill|Shōten-ji|ja|承天寺}} in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年(p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡}} in {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府市}}, the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a {{ill|Hōkyōintō|ja|宝篋印塔}} memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>
== References == <references />
== Sources == * 『尊卑分脈』 * 『亀山天皇紀』 * 『後宇多天皇紀』 * 渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年

'''Shōni Sukeyoshi''' (少弐 資能) was a [[samurai]] and retainer of the [[Kamakura shogunate]] active during the early to mid-[[Kamakura period]]. He was the second head of the {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏}}.
== Biography ==
During Sukeyoshi’s time, the {{ill|Muto clan|ja|武藤氏}} held the position of [[Dazai Shōni]], a role associated with governance in the {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|大宰府}} region. This responsibility led to the family adopting the surname {{ill|Shōni|ja|少弐氏}} starting from Sukeyoshi’s generation.
Sukeyoshi wielded significant power in northern Kyushu and served as the shogunate's {{ill|Chinzei Bugyō|ja|鎮西奉行}}. In [[Bun'ei]] 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the [[Yuan dynasty]].<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}} (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資}}. Despite his age, Sukeyoshi personally led the Kyushu defense forces and successfully repelled the invading Yuan forces.
In [[Kōan]] 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the {{ill|Second Mongol invasion of Japan|ja|弘安の役}}. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明}} at {{ill|Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)|ja|崇福寺}}, {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府}}.
In [[Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the {{ill|Shōten-ji|ja|承天寺}} in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年(p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡}} in {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府市}}, the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a {{ill|Hōkyōintō|ja|宝篋印塔}} memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>

'''Shōni Sukeyoshi''' (少弐 資能) was a [[samurai]] and retainer of the [[Kamakura shogunate]] active during the early to mid-[[Kamakura period]]. He was the second head of the {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏}}.

During Sukeyoshi’s time, the {{ill|Muto clan|ja|武藤氏}} held the position of [[Dazai Shōni]], a role associated with governance in the {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|大宰府}} region. This responsibility led to the family adopting the surname {{ill|Shōni|ja|少弐氏}} starting from Sukeyoshi’s generation.
Sukeyoshi wielded significant power in northern Kyushu and served as the shogunate's {{ill|Chinzei Bugyō|ja|鎮西奉行}}. In [[Bun'ei]] 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the [[Yuan dynasty]].<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}} (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資}}. Despite his age, Sukeyoshi personally led the Kyushu defense forces and successfully repelled the invading Yuan forces.
In [[Kōan]] 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the {{ill|Second Mongol invasion of Japan|ja|弘安の役}}. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明}} at {{ill|Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)|ja|崇福寺}}, {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府}}.
In [[Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the {{ill|Shōten-ji|ja|承天寺}} in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年(p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡}} in {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府市}}, the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a {{ill|Hōkyōintō|ja|宝篋印塔}} memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>

Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡}} in {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府市}}, the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a {{ill|Hōkyōintō|ja|宝篋印塔}} memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>

In [[Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the {{ill|Shōten-ji|ja|承天寺}} in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年(p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡}} in {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府市}}, the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a {{ill|Hōkyōintō|ja|宝篋印塔}} memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>

[[Category:Https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[Category:Https://ko.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80%EC%9A%94%EC%8B%9C]] [[zh:%E5%B0%91%E8%B4%B0%E8%B5%84%E8%83%BD]] [[ja:%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E8%B3%87%E8%83%BD]] {{Infobox samurai | name = Shōni Sukeyoshi (少弐 資能) | image = | imagesize = | caption = | era = Early to mid-[[Kamakura period]] | birth_date = [[Kenkyū]] 9 (1198) | death_date = [[Kōan]] 4, intercalary [[7th month]], [[13th day (lunar calendar)|13th day]] (August 28, 1281) | posthumous_name = Kakue (覚恵; Dharma name) | alias = Muto Sukeyoshi | rank = [[Dazai Shōni|Assistant Governor of Dazaifu]] | allegiance = [[Kamakura shogunate]] | lord = [[Fujiwara no Yoritsune|Yoritsune]], [[Fujiwara no Yoritsugu|Yoritsugu]], [[Prince Munetaka]], [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}} | siblings = Sukeyoshi, {{ill|Muto Yorishige|ja|武藤頼茂}}, {{ill|Muto Tameyori|ja|武藤為頼}}, {{ill|Muto Yorimoto|ja|武藤頼基}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資}}, {{ill|Shōni Kagesue|ja|少弐景資}}, {{ill|Shōni Morisue|ja|少弐盛資}}<ref name="sonpibunmyaku">『尊卑分脈』第2篇・391頁。</ref>, {{ill|Shōni Munetsune|ja|少弐宗資}}? | notable = }}
'''Shōni Sukeyoshi''' (少弐 資能) was a [[samurai]] and retainer of the [[Kamakura shogunate]] active during the early to mid-[[Kamakura period]]. He was the second head of the {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏}}.
== Biography ==
During Sukeyoshi’s time, the {{ill|Muto clan|ja|武藤氏}} held the position of [[Dazai Shōni]], a role associated with governance in the {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|大宰府}} region. This responsibility led to the family adopting the surname {{ill|Shōni|ja|少弐氏}} starting from Sukeyoshi’s generation.
Sukeyoshi wielded significant power in northern Kyushu and served as the shogunate's {{ill|Chinzei Bugyō|ja|鎮西奉行}}. In [[Bun'ei]] 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the [[Yuan dynasty]].<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}} (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資}}. Despite his age, Sukeyoshi personally led the Kyushu defense forces and successfully repelled the invading Yuan forces.
In [[Kōan]] 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the {{ill|Second Mongol invasion of Japan|ja|弘安の役}}. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明}} at {{ill|Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)|ja|崇福寺}}, {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府}}.
In [[Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the {{ill|Shōten-ji|ja|承天寺}} in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年(p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡}} in {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府市}}, the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a {{ill|Hōkyōintō|ja|宝篋印塔}} memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>

In [[Kōan]] 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the {{ill|Second Mongol invasion of Japan|ja|弘安の役}}. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明}} at {{ill|Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)|ja|崇福寺}}, {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府}}.
In [[Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the {{ill|Shōten-ji|ja|承天寺}} in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年(p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡}} in {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府市}}, the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a {{ill|Hōkyōintō|ja|宝篋印塔}} memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>

'''Shōni Sukeyoshi''' (少弐 資能) was a [[samurai]] and retainer of the [[Kamakura shogunate]] active during the early to mid-[[Kamakura period]]. He was the second head of the {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏}}.

During Sukeyoshi’s time, the {{ill|Muto clan|ja|武藤氏}} held the position of [[Dazai Shōni]], a role associated with governance in the {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|大宰府}} region. This responsibility led to the family adopting the surname {{ill|Shōni|ja|少弐氏}} starting from Sukeyoshi’s generation.
Sukeyoshi wielded significant power in northern Kyushu and served as the shogunate's {{ill|Chinzei Bugyō|ja|鎮西奉行}}. Despite his age, Sukeyoshi personally led the Kyushu defense forces and successfully repelled the invading Yuan forces.
In [[Kōan]] 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the {{ill|Second Mongol invasion of Japan|ja|弘安の役}}. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明}} at {{ill|Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)|ja|崇福寺}}, {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府}}.

'''Shōni Sukeyoshi''' (少弐 資能) was a [[samurai]] and retainer of the [[Kamakura shogunate]] active during the early to mid-[[Kamakura period]]. He was the second head of the {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏}}.
== Biography ==
During Sukeyoshi’s time, the {{ill|Muto clan|ja|武藤氏}} held the position of [[Dazai Shōni]], a role associated with governance in the {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|大宰府}} region. This responsibility led to the family adopting the surname {{ill|Shōni|ja|少弐氏}} starting from Sukeyoshi’s generation.
Sukeyoshi wielded significant power in northern Kyushu and served as the shogunate's {{ill|Chinzei Bugyō|ja|鎮西奉行}}. In [[Bun'ei]] 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the [[Yuan dynasty]].<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}} (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資}}. Despite his age, Sukeyoshi personally led the Kyushu defense forces and successfully repelled the invading Yuan forces.
In [[Kōan]] 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the {{ill|Second Mongol invasion of Japan|ja|弘安の役}}. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明}} at {{ill|Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)|ja|崇福寺}}, {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府}}.
In [[Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the {{ill|Shōten-ji|ja|承天寺}} in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年(p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡}} in {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府市}}, the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a {{ill|Hōkyōintō|ja|宝篋印塔}} memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>

{{ill|Shōni Sukenaga|ja|少弐資能}}
[[Category:Https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[Category:Https://ko.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80%EC%9A%94%EC%8B%9C]] [[zh:%E5%B0%91%E8%B4%B0%E8%B5%84%E8%83%BD]] [[ja:%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E8%B3%87%E8%83%BD]] {{Infobox samurai | name = Shōni Sukeyoshi (少弐 資能) | image = | imagesize = | caption = | era = Early to mid-[[Kamakura period]] | birth_date = [[Kenkyū]] 9 (qq98) | death_date = [[Kōan]] 4, intercalary [[7th month]], [[13th day (lunar calendar)|13th day]] (August 28, 1281) | posthumous_name = Kakue (覚恵; Dharma name) | alias = Muto Sukeyoshi | rank = [[Dazai Shōni|Assistant Governor of Dazaifu]] | allegiance = [[Kamakura shogunate]] | lord = [[Fujiwara no Yoritsune|Yoritsune]], [[Fujiwara no Yoritsugu|Yoritsugu]], [[Prince Munetaka]], [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}} | siblings = Sukeyoshi, {{ill|Muto Yorishige|ja|武藤頼茂}}, {{ill|Muto Tameyori|ja|武藤為頼}}, {{ill|Muto Yorimoto|ja|武藤頼基}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資}}, {{ill|Shōni Kagesue|ja|少弐景資}}, {{ill|Shōni Morisue|ja|少弐盛資}}<ref name="sonpibunmyaku">『尊卑分脈』第2篇・391頁。</ref>, {{ill|Shōni Munetsune|ja|少弐宗資}}? | notable = }}
'''Shōni Sukeyoshi''' (少弐 資能) was a [[samurai]] and retainer of the [[Kamakura shogunate]] active during the early to mid-[[Kamakura period]]. He was the second head of the {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏}}.
== Biography ==
During Sukeyoshi’s time, the {{ill|Muto clan|ja|武藤氏}} held the position of [[Dazai Shōni]], a role associated with governance in the {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|大宰府}} region. This responsibility led to the family adopting the surname {{ill|Shōni|ja|少弐氏}} starting from Sukeyoshi’s generation.
Sukeyoshi wielded significant power in northern Kyushu and served as the shogunate's {{ill|Chinzei Bugyō|ja|鎮西奉行}}. In [[Bun'ei]] 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the [[Yuan dynasty]].<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}} (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資}}. Despite his age, Sukeyoshi personally led the Kyushu defense forces and successfully repelled the invading Yuan forces.
In [[Kōan]] 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the {{ill|Second Mongol invasion of Japan|ja|弘安の役}}. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明}} at {{ill|Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)|ja|崇福寺}}, {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府}}.
In [[Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the {{ill|Shōten-ji|ja|承天寺}} in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年(p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡}} in {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府市}}, the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a {{ill|Hōkyōintō|ja|宝篋印塔}} memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>

In [[Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the {{ill|Shōten-ji|ja|承天寺}} in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年(p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡}} in {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府市}}, the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a {{ill|Hōkyōintō|ja|宝篋印塔}} memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>

[[Category:Https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80%EC%9A%94%EC%8B%9C]] [[zh:%E5%B0%91%E8%B4%B0%E8%B5%84%E8%83%BD]] [[ja:%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E8%B3%87%E8%83%BD]] {{Infobox samurai | name = Shōni Sukeyoshi (少弐 資能) | image = | imagesize = | caption = | era = Early to mid-[[Kamakura period]] | birth_date = [[Kenkyū]] 9 (qq98) | death_date = [[Kōan]] 4, intercalary [[7th month]], [[13th day (lunar calendar)|13th day]] (August 28, 1281) | posthumous_name = Kakue (覚恵; Dharma name) | alias = Muto Sukeyoshi | rank = [[Dazai Shōni|Assistant Governor of Dazaifu]] | allegiance = [[Kamakura shogunate]] | lord = [[Fujiwara no Yoritsune|Yoritsune]], [[Fujiwara no Yoritsugu|Yoritsugu]], [[Prince Munetaka]], [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}} | siblings = Sukeyoshi, {{ill|Muto Yorishige|ja|武藤頼茂}}, {{ill|Muto Tameyori|ja|武藤為頼}}, {{ill|Muto Yorimoto|ja|武藤頼基}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資}}, {{ill|Shōni Kagesue|ja|少弐景資}}, {{ill|Shōni Morisue|ja|少弐盛資}}<ref name="sonpibunmyaku">『尊卑分脈』第2篇・391頁。</ref>, {{ill|Shōni Munetsune|ja|少弐宗資}}? | notable = }}
'''Shōni Sukeyoshi''' (少弐 資能) was a [[samurai]] and retainer of the [[Kamakura shogunate]] active during the early to mid-[[Kamakura period]]. He was the second head of the {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏}}.
== Biography ==
During Sukeyoshi’s time, the {{ill|Muto clan|ja|武藤氏}} held the position of [[Dazai Shōni]], a role associated with governance in the {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|大宰府}} region. This responsibility led to the family adopting the surname {{ill|Shōni|ja|少弐氏}} starting from Sukeyoshi’s generation.
Sukeyoshi wielded significant power in northern Kyushu and served as the shogunate's {{ill|Chinzei Bugyō|ja|鎮西奉行}}. In [[Bun'ei]] 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the [[Yuan dynasty]].<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}} (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資}}. Despite his age, Sukeyoshi personally led the Kyushu defense forces and successfully repelled the invading Yuan forces.
In [[Kōan]] 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the {{ill|Second Mongol invasion of Japan|ja|弘安の役}}. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明}} at {{ill|Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)|ja|崇福寺}}, {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府}}.
In [[Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the {{ill|Shōten-ji|ja|承天寺}} in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年(p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡}} in {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府市}}, the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a {{ill|Hōkyōintō|ja|宝篋印塔}} memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>

In [[Kōan]] 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the {{ill|Second Mongol invasion of Japan|ja|弘安の役}}. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明}} at {{ill|Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)|ja|崇福寺}}, {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府}}.
In [[Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the {{ill|Shōten-ji|ja|承天寺}} in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年(p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡}} in {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府市}}, the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a {{ill|Hōkyōintō|ja|宝篋印塔}} memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>

'''Shōni Sukeyoshi''' (少弐 資能) was a [[samurai]] and retainer of the [[Kamakura shogunate]] active during the early to mid-[[Kamakura period]]. He was the second head of the {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏}}.
== Biography ==
During Sukeyoshi’s time, the {{ill|Muto clan|ja|武藤氏}} held the position of [[Dazai Shōni]], a role associated with governance in the {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|大宰府}} region. This responsibility led to the family adopting the surname {{ill|Shōni|ja|少弐氏}} starting from Sukeyoshi’s generation.
Sukeyoshi wielded significant power in northern Kyushu and served as the shogunate's {{ill|Chinzei Bugyō|ja|鎮西奉行}}. In [[Bun'ei]] 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the [[Yuan dynasty]].<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}} (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資}}. Despite his age, Sukeyoshi personally led the Kyushu defense forces and successfully repelled the invading Yuan forces.
In [[Kōan]] 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the {{ill|Second Mongol invasion of Japan|ja|弘安の役}}. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明}} at {{ill|Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)|ja|崇福寺}}, {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府}}.
In [[Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the {{ill|Shōten-ji|ja|承天寺}} in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年(p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡}} in {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府市}}, the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a {{ill|Hōkyōintō|ja|宝篋印塔}} memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>

{{ill|Shōni Sukenaga|ja|少弐資能}}
[[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80%EC%9A%94%EC%8B%9C]] [[zh:%E5%B0%91%E8%B4%B0%E8%B5%84%E8%83%BD]] [[ja:%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E8%B3%87%E8%83%BD]] {{Infobox samurai | name = Shōni Sukeyoshi (少弐 資能) | image = | imagesize = | caption = | era = Early to mid-[[Kamakura period]] | birth_date = [[Kenkyū]] 9 (qq98) | death_date = [[Kōan]] 4, intercalary [[7th month]], [[13th day (lunar calendar)|13th day]] (August 28, 1281) | posthumous_name = Kakue (覚恵; Dharma name) | alias = Muto Sukeyoshi | rank = [[Dazai Shōni|Assistant Governor of Dazaifu]] | allegiance = [[Kamakura shogunate]] | lord = [[Fujiwara no Yoritsune|Yoritsune]], [[Fujiwara no Yoritsugu|Yoritsugu]], [[Prince Munetaka]], [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}} | siblings = Sukeyoshi, {{ill|Muto Yorishige|ja|武藤頼茂}}, {{ill|Muto Tameyori|ja|武藤為頼}}, {{ill|Muto Yorimoto|ja|武藤頼基}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資}}, {{ill|Shōni Kagesue|ja|少弐景資}}, {{ill|Shōni Morisue|ja|少弐盛資}}<ref name="sonpibunmyaku">『尊卑分脈』第2篇・391頁。</ref>, {{ill|Shōni Munetsune|ja|少弐宗資}}? | notable = }}
'''Shōni Sukeyoshi''' (少弐 資能) was a [[samurai]] and retainer of the [[Kamakura shogunate]] active during the early to mid-[[Kamakura period]]. He was the second head of the {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏}}.
== Biography ==
During Sukeyoshi’s time, the {{ill|Muto clan|ja|武藤氏}} held the position of [[Dazai Shōni]], a role associated with governance in the {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|大宰府}} region. This responsibility led to the family adopting the surname {{ill|Shōni|ja|少弐氏}} starting from Sukeyoshi’s generation.
Sukeyoshi wielded significant power in northern Kyushu and served as the shogunate's {{ill|Chinzei Bugyō|ja|鎮西奉行}}. In [[Bun'ei]] 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the [[Yuan dynasty]].<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}} (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資}}. Despite his age, Sukeyoshi personally led the Kyushu defense forces and successfully repelled the invading Yuan forces.
In [[Kōan]] 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the {{ill|Second Mongol invasion of Japan|ja|弘安の役}}. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明}} at {{ill|Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)|ja|崇福寺}}, {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府}}.
In [[Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the {{ill|Shōten-ji|ja|承天寺}} in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年(p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡}} in {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府市}}, the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a {{ill|Hōkyōintō|ja|宝篋印塔}} memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>

'''Shōni Sukeyoshi''' (少弐 資能) was a [[samurai]] and retainer of the [[Kamakura shogunate]] active during the early to mid-[[Kamakura period]]. He was the second head of the {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏}}.

During Sukeyoshi’s time, the {{ill|Muto clan|ja|武藤氏}} held the position of [[Dazai Shōni]], a role associated with governance in the {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|大宰府}} region. This responsibility led to the family adopting the surname {{ill|Shōni|ja|少弐氏}} starting from Sukeyoshi’s generation.

In [[Kōan]] 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the {{ill|Second Mongol invasion of Japan|ja|弘安の役}}. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明}} at {{ill|Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)|ja|崇福寺}}, {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府}}.
In [[Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the {{ill|Shōten-ji|ja|承天寺}} in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年(p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡}} in {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府市}}, the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a {{ill|Hōkyōintō|ja|宝篋印塔}} memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>

{{ill|Shōni Sukenaga|ja|少弐資能}}
[[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80%EC%9A%94%EC%8B%9C]] [[zh:%E5%B0%91%E8%B4%B0%E8%B5%84%E8%83%BD]] [[ja:%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E8%B3%87%E8%83%BD]] {{Infobox samurai | name = Shōni Sukeyoshi (少弐 資能) | image = | imagesize = | caption = | era = Early to mid-[[Kamakura period]] | birth_date = [[Kenkyū]] 9 (qq98) | death_date = [[Kōan]] 4, intercalary [[7th month]], [[13th day (lunar calendar)|13th day]] (August 28, 1281) | posthumous_name = Kakue (覚恵; Dharma name) | alias = Muto Sukeyoshi | rank = [[Dazai Shōni|Assistant Governor of Dazaifu]] | allegiance = [[Kamakura shogunate]] | lord = [[Fujiwara no Yoritsune|Yoritsune]], [[Fujiwara no Yoritsugu|Yoritsugu]], [[Prince Munetaka]], [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}} | siblings = Sukeyoshi, {{ill|Muto Yorishige|ja|武藤頼茂}}, {{ill|Muto Tameyori|ja|武藤為頼}}, {{ill|Muto Yorimoto|ja|武藤頼基}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資}}, {{ill|Shōni Kagesue|ja|少弐景資}}, {{ill|Shōni Morisue|ja|少弐盛資}}<ref name="sonpibunmyaku">『尊卑分脈』第2篇・391頁。</ref>, {{ill|Shōni Munetsune|ja|少弐宗資}}? | notable = }}
'''Shōni Sukeyoshi''' (少弐 資能) was a [[samurai]] and retainer of the [[Kamakura shogunate]] active during the early to mid-[[Kamakura period]]. He was the second head of the {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏}}.
== Biography ==
During Sukeyoshi’s time, the {{ill|Muto clan|ja|武藤氏}} held the position of [[Dazai Shōni]], a role associated with governance in the {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|大宰府}} region. This responsibility led to the family adopting the surname {{ill|Shōni|ja|少弐氏}} starting from Sukeyoshi’s generation.
Sukeyoshi wielded significant power in northern Kyushu and served as the shogunate's {{ill|Chinzei Bugyō|ja|鎮西奉行}}. In [[Bun'ei]] 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the [[Yuan dynasty]].<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}} (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資}}. Despite his age, Sukeyoshi personally led the Kyushu defense forces and successfully repelled the invading Yuan forces.
In [[Kōan]] 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the {{ill|Second Mongol invasion of Japan|ja|弘安の役}}. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明}} at {{ill|Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)|ja|崇福寺}}, {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府}}.
In [[Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the {{ill|Shōten-ji|ja|承天寺}} in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年(p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡}} in {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府市}}, the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a {{ill|Hōkyōintō|ja|宝篋印塔}} memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>

{{ill|Shōni Sukenaga|ja|少弐資能}}
[[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80%EC%9A%94%EC%8B%9C]] [[zh:%E5%B0%91%E8%B4%B0%E8%B5%84%E8%83%BD]] [[ja:%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E8%B3%87%E8%83%BD]] {{Infobox samurai | name = Shōni Sukeyoshi (少弐 資能) | image = | imagesize = | caption = | era = Early to mid-[[Kamakura period]] | birth_date = [[Kenkyū]] 9 (qq98) | death_date = [[Kōan]] 4, intercalary [[7th month]], [[13th day (lunar calendar)|13th day]] (August 28, 1281) | posthumous_name = Kakue (覚恵; Dharma name) | alias = Muto Sukeyoshi | rank = [[Dazai Shōni|Assistant Governor of Dazaifu]] | allegiance = [[Kamakura shogunate]] | lord = [[Fujiwara no Yoritsune|Yoritsune]], [[Fujiwara no Yoritsugu|Yoritsugu]], [[Prince Munetaka]], [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}} | siblings = Sukeyoshi, {{ill|Muto Yorishige|ja|武藤頼茂}}, {{ill|Muto Tameyori|ja|武藤為頼}}, {{ill|Muto Yorimoto|ja|武藤頼基}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資}}, {{ill|Shōni Kagesue|ja|少弐景資}}, {{ill|Shōni Morisue|ja|少弐盛資}}<ref name="sonpibunmyaku">『尊卑分脈』第2篇・391頁。</ref>, {{ill|Shōni Munetsune|ja|少弐宗資}}? | notable = }}
'''Shōni Sukeyoshi''' (少弐 資能) was a [[samurai]] and retainer of the [[Kamakura shogunate]] active during the early to mid-[[Kamakura period]]. He was the second head of the {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏}}.
== Biography ==
During Sukeyoshi’s time, the {{ill|Muto clan|ja|武藤氏}} held the position of [[Dazai Shōni]], a role associated with governance in the {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|大宰府}} region. This responsibility led to the family adopting the surname {{ill|Shōni|ja|少弐氏}} starting from Sukeyoshi’s generation.
Sukeyoshi wielded significant power in northern Kyushu and served as the shogunate's {{ill|Chinzei Bugyō|ja|鎮西奉行}}. In [[Bun'ei]] 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the [[Yuan dynasty]].<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}} (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資}}. Despite his age, Sukeyoshi personally led the Kyushu defense forces and successfully repelled the invading Yuan forces.
In [[Kōan]] 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the {{ill|Second Mongol invasion of Japan|ja|弘安の役}}. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明}} at {{ill|Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)|ja|崇福寺}}, {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府}}.
In [[Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the {{ill|Shōten-ji|ja|承天寺}} in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年(p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡}} in {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府市}}, the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a {{ill|Hōkyōintō|ja|宝篋印塔}} memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>

{{ill|Shōni Sukenaga|ja|少弐資能}}
[[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80%EC%9A%94%EC%8B%9C]] [[zh:%E5%B0%91%E8%B4%B0%E8%B5%84%E8%83%BD]] [[ja:%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E8%B3%87%E8%83%BD]] {{Infobox samurai | name = Shōni Sukeyoshi (少弐 資能) | image = | imagesize = | caption = | era = Early to mid-[[Kamakura period]] | birth_date = [[Kenkyū]] 9 (qq98) | death_date = [[Kōan]] 4, intercalary [[7th month]], [[13th day (lunar calendar)|13th day]] (August 28, 1281) | posthumous_name = Kakue (覚恵; Dharma name) | alias = Muto Sukeyoshi | rank = [[Dazai Shōni|Assistant Governor of Dazaifu]] | allegiance = [[Kamakura shogunate]] | lord = [[Fujiwara no Yoritsune|Yoritsune]], [[Fujiwara no Yoritsugu|Yoritsugu]], [[Prince Munetaka]], [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}} | siblings = Sukeyoshi, {{ill|Muto Yorishige|ja|武藤頼茂}}, {{ill|Muto Tameyori|ja|武藤為頼}}, {{ill|Muto Yorimoto|ja|武藤頼基}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資}}, {{ill|Shōni Kagesue|ja|少弐景資}}, {{ill|Shōni Morisue|ja|少弐盛資}}<ref name="sonpibunmyaku">『尊卑分脈』第2篇・391頁。</ref>, {{ill|Shōni Munetsune|ja|少弐宗資}}? | notable = }}
'''Shōni Sukeyoshi''' (少弐 資能) was a [[samurai]] and retainer of the [[Kamakura shogunate]] active during the early to mid-[[Kamakura period]]. He was the second head of the {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏}}.
== Biography ==
During Sukeyoshi’s time, the {{ill|Muto clan|ja|武藤氏}} held the position of [[Dazai Shōni]], a role associated with governance in the {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|大宰府}} region. This responsibility led to the family adopting the surname {{ill|Shōni|ja|少弐氏}} starting from Sukeyoshi’s generation.
Sukeyoshi wielded significant power in northern Kyushu and served as the shogunate's {{ill|Chinzei Bugyō|ja|鎮西奉行}}. In [[Bun'ei]] 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the [[Yuan dynasty]].<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}} (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資}}. Despite his age, Sukeyoshi personally led the Kyushu defense forces and successfully repelled the invading Yuan forces.
In [[Kōan]] 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the {{ill|Second Mongol invasion of Japan|ja|弘安の役}}. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明}} at {{ill|Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)|ja|崇福寺}}, {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府}}.
In [[Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the {{ill|Shōten-ji|ja|承天寺}} in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年(p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡}} in {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府市}}, the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a {{ill|Hōkyōintō|ja|宝篋印塔}} memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>

[[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80%EC%9A%94%EC%8B%9C]] [[zh:%E5%B0%91%E8%B4%B0%E8%B5%84%E8%83%BD]] [[ja:%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E8%B3%87%E8%83%BD]] {{Infobox samurai | name = Shōni Sukeyoshi (少弐 資能) | image = | imagesize = | caption = | era = Early to mid-[[Kamakura period]] | birth_date = [[Kenkyū]] 9 (1198) | death_date = [[Kōan]] 4, intercalary [[7th month]], [[13th day (lunar calendar)|13th day]] (August 28, 1281) | posthumous_name = Kakue (覚恵; Dharma name) | alias = Muto Sukeyoshi | rank = [[Dazai Shōni|Assistant Governor of Dazaifu]] | allegiance = [[Kamakura shogunate]] | lord = [[Fujiwara no Yoritsune|Yoritsune]], [[Fujiwara no Yoritsugu|Yoritsugu]], [[Prince Munetaka]], [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}} | siblings = Sukeyoshi, {{ill|Muto Yorishige|ja|武藤頼茂}}, {{ill|Muto Tameyori|ja|武藤為頼}}, {{ill|Muto Yorimoto|ja|武藤頼基}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資}}, {{ill|Shōni Kagesue|ja|少弐景資}}, {{ill|Shōni Morisue|ja|少弐盛資}}<ref name="sonpibunmyaku">『尊卑分脈』第2篇・391頁。</ref>, {{ill|Shōni Munetsune|ja|少弐宗資}}? | notable = }}
'''Shōni Sukeyoshi''' (少弐 資能) was a [[samurai]] and retainer of the [[Kamakura shogunate]] active during the early to mid-[[Kamakura period]]. He was the second head of the {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏}}.
== Biography ==
During Sukeyoshi’s time, the {{ill|Muto clan|ja|武藤氏}} held the position of [[Dazai Shōni]], a role associated with governance in the {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|大宰府}} region. This responsibility led to the family adopting the surname {{ill|Shōni|ja|少弐氏}} starting from Sukeyoshi’s generation.
Sukeyoshi wielded significant power in northern Kyushu and served as the shogunate's {{ill|Chinzei Bugyō|ja|鎮西奉行}}. In [[Bun'ei]] 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the [[Yuan dynasty]].<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}} (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資}}. Despite his age, Sukeyoshi personally led the Kyushu defense forces and successfully repelled the invading Yuan forces.
In [[Kōan]] 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the {{ill|Second Mongol invasion of Japan|ja|弘安の役}}. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明}} at {{ill|Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)|ja|崇福寺}}, {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府}}.
In [[Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the {{ill|Shōten-ji|ja|承天寺}} in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年(p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡}} in {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府市}}, the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a {{ill|Hōkyōintō|ja|宝篋印塔}} memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>
== References == <references />

[[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80%EC%9A%94%EC%8B%9C]] [[zh:%E5%B0%91%E8%B4%B0%E8%B5%84%E8%83%BD]] [[ja:%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E8%B3%87%E8%83%BD]] {{Infobox samurai | name = Shōni Sukeyoshi (少弐 資能) | image = | imagesize = | caption = | era = Early to mid-[[Kamakura period]] | birth_date = [[Kenkyū]] 9 (1198) | death_date = [[Kōan]] 4, intercalary [[7th month]], [[13th day (lunar calendar)|13th day]] (August 28, 1281) | posthumous_name = Kakue (覚恵; Dharma name) | alias = Muto Sukeyoshi | rank = [[Dazai Shōni|Assistant Governor of Dazaifu]] | allegiance = [[Kamakura shogunate]] | lord = [[Fujiwara no Yoritsune|Yoritsune]], [[Fujiwara no Yoritsugu|Yoritsugu]], [[Prince Munetaka]], [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}} | siblings = Sukeyoshi, {{ill|Muto Yorishige|ja|武藤頼茂}}, {{ill|Muto Tameyori|ja|武藤為頼}}, {{ill|Muto Yorimoto|ja|武藤頼基}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資}}, {{ill|Shōni Kagesue|ja|少弐景資}}, {{ill|Shōni Morisue|ja|少弐盛資}}<ref name="sonpibunmyaku">『尊卑分脈』第2篇・391頁。</ref>, {{ill|Shōni Munetsune|ja|少弐宗資}}? | notable = }}
'''Shōni Sukeyoshi''' (少弐 資能) was a [[samurai]] and retainer of the [[Kamakura shogunate]] active during the early to mid-[[Kamakura period]]. He was the second head of the {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏}}.
== Biography ==
During Sukeyoshi’s time, the {{ill|Muto clan|ja|武藤氏}} held the position of [[Dazai Shōni]], a role associated with governance in the {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|大宰府}} region. This responsibility led to the family adopting the surname {{ill|Shōni|ja|少弐氏}} starting from Sukeyoshi’s generation.
Sukeyoshi wielded significant power in northern Kyushu and served as the shogunate's {{ill|Chinzei Bugyō|ja|鎮西奉行}}. In [[Bun'ei]] 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the [[Yuan dynasty]].<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}} (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資}}. Despite his age, Sukeyoshi personally led the Kyushu defense forces and successfully repelled the invading Yuan forces.
In [[Kōan]] 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the {{ill|Second Mongol invasion of Japan|ja|弘安の役}}. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明}} at {{ill|Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)|ja|崇福寺}}, {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府}}.
In [[Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the {{ill|Shōten-ji|ja|承天寺}} in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年(p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡}} in {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府市}}, the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a {{ill|Hōkyōintō|ja|宝篋印塔}} memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>
