[[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80%EC%9A%94%EC%8B%9C]] [[zh:%E5%B0%91%E8%B4%B0%E8%B5%84%E8%83%BD]] [[ja:%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E8%B3%87%E8%83%BD]] {{Infobox samurai | name = Shōni Sukeyoshi (少弐 資能) | image = | imagesize = | caption = | era = Early to mid-[[Kamakura period]] | birth_date = [[Kenkyū]] 9 (1198) | death_date = [[Kōan]] 4, intercalary [[7th month]], [[13th day (lunar calendar)|13th day]] (August 28, 1281) | posthumous_name = Kakue (覚恵; Dharma name) | alias = Muto Sukeyoshi | rank = [[Dazai Shōni|Assistant Governor of Dazaifu]] | allegiance = [[Kamakura shogunate]] | lord = [[Fujiwara no Yoritsune|Yoritsune]], [[Fujiwara no Yoritsugu|Yoritsugu]], [[Prince Munetaka]], [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}} | siblings = Sukeyoshi, {{ill|Muto Yorishige|ja|武藤頼茂}}, {{ill|Muto Tameyori|ja|武藤為頼}}, {{ill|Muto Yorimoto|ja|武藤頼基}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資}}, {{ill|Shōni Kagesue|ja|少弐景資}}, {{ill|Shōni Morisue|ja|少弐盛資}}<ref name="sonpibunmyaku">『尊卑分脈』第2篇・391頁。</ref>, {{ill|Shōni Munetsune|ja|少弐宗資}}? | notable = }}
'''Shōni Sukeyoshi''' (少弐 資能) was a [[samurai]] and retainer of the [[Kamakura shogunate]] active during the early to mid-[[Kamakura period]]. He was the second head of the {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏}}.
== Biography ==
During Sukeyoshi’s time, the {{ill|Muto clan|ja|武藤氏}} held the position of [[Dazai Shōni]], a role associated with governance in the {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|大宰府}} region. This responsibility led to the family adopting the surname {{ill|Shōni|ja|少弐氏}} starting from Sukeyoshi’s generation.
Sukeyoshi wielded significant power in northern Kyushu and served as the shogunate's {{ill|Chinzei Bugyō|ja|鎮西奉行}}. In [[Bun'ei]] 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the [[Yuan dynasty]].<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}} (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資}}. Despite his age, Sukeyoshi personally led the Kyushu defense forces and successfully repelled the invading Yuan forces.
In [[Kōan]] 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the {{ill|Second Mongol invasion of Japan|ja|弘安の役}}. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明}} at {{ill|Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)|ja|崇福寺}}, {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府}}.
In [[Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the {{ill|Shōten-ji|ja|承天寺}} in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989th year (p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡}} in {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府市}}, the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a {{ill|Hōkyōintō|ja|宝篋印塔|1=Hōkyōintō}} memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>

[[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80%EC%9A%94%EC%8B%9C]] [[zh:%E5%B0%91%E8%B4%B0%E8%B5%84%E8%83%BD]] [[ja:%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E8%B3%87%E8%83%BD]] {{Infobox samurai | name = Shōni Sukeyoshi (少弐 資能) | image = | imagesize = | caption = | era = Early to mid-[[Kamakura period]] | birth_date = [[Kenkyū]] 9 (1198) | death_date = [[Kōan]] 4, intercalary [[7th month]], [[13th day (lunar calendar)|13th day]] (August 28, 1281) | posthumous_name = Kakue (覚恵; Dharma name) | alias = Muto Sukeyoshi | rank = [[Dazai Shōni|Assistant Governor of Dazaifu]] | allegiance = [[Kamakura shogunate]] | lord = [[Fujiwara no Yoritsune|Yoritsune]], [[Fujiwara no Yoritsugu|Yoritsugu]], [[Prince Munetaka]], [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}} | siblings = Sukeyoshi, {{ill|Muto Yorishige|ja|武藤頼茂}}, {{ill|Muto Tameyori|ja|武藤為頼}}, {{ill|Muto Yorimoto|ja|武藤頼基}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資}}, {{ill|Shōni Kagesue|ja|少弐景資}}, {{ill|Shōni Morisue|ja|少弐盛資}}<ref name="sonpibunmyaku">『尊卑分脈』第2篇・391頁。</ref>, {{ill|Shōni Munetsune|ja|少弐宗資}}? | notable = }}
'''Shōni Sukeyoshi''' (少弐 資能) was a [[samurai]] and retainer of the [[Kamakura shogunate]] active during the early to mid-[[Kamakura period]]. He was the second head of the {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏}}.
== Biography ==
During Sukeyoshi’s time, the {{ill|Muto clan|ja|武藤氏}} held the position of [[Dazai Shōni]], a role associated with governance in the {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|大宰府}} region. This responsibility led to the family adopting the surname {{ill|Shōni|ja|少弐氏}} starting from Sukeyoshi’s generation.
Sukeyoshi wielded significant power in northern Kyushu and served as the shogunate's {{ill|Chinzei Bugyō|ja|鎮西奉行}}. In [[Bun'ei]] 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the [[Yuan dynasty]].<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}} (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資}}. Despite his age, Sukeyoshi personally led the Kyushu defense forces and successfully repelled the invading Yuan forces.
In [[Kōan]] 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the {{ill|Second Mongol invasion of Japan|ja|弘安の役}}. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明}} at {{ill|Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)|ja|崇福寺}}, {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府}}.
In [[Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the {{ill|Shōten-ji|ja|承天寺}} in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989th year (p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡}} in {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府市}}, the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a {{ill|Hōkyōintō|ja|宝篋印塔|1=Hōkyōintō}} memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>

{{Infobox samurai | name = Shōni Sukeyoshi (少弐 資能) | image = | imagesize = | caption = | era = Early to mid-[[Kamakura period]] | birth_date = [[Kenkyū]] 9 (1198) | death_date = [[Kōan]] 4, intercalary [[7th month]], [[13th day (lunar calendar)|13th day]] (August 28, 1281) | posthumous_name = Kakue (覚恵; Dharma name) | alias = Muto Sukeyoshi | rank = [[Dazai Shōni|Assistant Governor of Dazaifu]] | allegiance = [[Kamakura shogunate]] | lord = [[Fujiwara no Yoritsune|Yoritsune]], [[Fujiwara no Yoritsugu|Yoritsugu]], [[Prince Munetaka]], [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}} | siblings = Sukeyoshi, {{ill|Muto Yorishige|ja|武藤頼茂}}, {{ill|Muto Tameyori|ja|武藤為頼}}, {{ill|Muto Yorimoto|ja|武藤頼基}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資}}, {{ill|Shōni Kagesue|ja|少弐景資}}, {{ill|Shōni Morisue|ja|少弐盛資}}<ref name="sonpibunmyaku">『尊卑分脈』第2篇・391頁。</ref>, {{ill|Shōni Munetsune|ja|少弐宗資}}? | notable = }}
'''Shōni Sukeyoshi''' (少弐 資能) was a [[samurai]] and retainer of the [[Kamakura shogunate]] active during the early to mid-[[Kamakura period]]. He was the second head of the {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏}}.
== Biography ==
During Sukeyoshi’s time, the {{ill|Muto clan|ja|武藤氏}} held the position of [[Dazai Shōni]], a role associated with governance in the {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|大宰府}} region. This responsibility led to the family adopting the surname {{ill|Shōni|ja|少弐氏}} starting from Sukeyoshi’s generation.
Sukeyoshi wielded significant power in northern Kyushu and served as the shogunate's {{ill|Chinzei Bugyō|ja|鎮西奉行}}. In [[Bun'ei]] 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the [[Yuan dynasty]].<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}} (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資}}. Despite his age, Sukeyoshi personally led the Kyushu defense forces and successfully repelled the invading Yuan forces.
In [[Kōan]] 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the {{ill|Second Mongol invasion of Japan|ja|弘安の役}}. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明}} at {{ill|Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)|ja|崇福寺}}, {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府}}.
In [[Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the {{ill|Shōten-ji|ja|承天寺}} in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989th year (p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡}} in {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府市}}, the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a {{ill|Hōkyōintō|ja|宝篋印塔|1=Hōkyōintō}} memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>
== References == <references />

{{ill|Shōni Sukenaga|ja|少弐資能|12=draft}}
{{Infobox samurai | name = Shōni Sukeyoshi (少弐 資能) | image = | imagesize = | caption = | era = Early to mid-[[Kamakura period]] | birth_date = [[Kenkyū]] 9 (1198) | death_date = [[Kōan]] 4, intercalary [[7th month]], [[13th day (lunar calendar)|13th day]] (August 28, 1281) | posthumous_name = Kakue (覚恵; Dharma name) | alias = Muto Sukeyoshi | rank = [[Dazai Shōni|Assistant Governor of Dazaifu]] | allegiance = [[Kamakura shogunate]] | lord = [[Fujiwara no Yoritsune|Yoritsune]], [[Fujiwara no Yoritsugu|Yoritsugu]], [[Prince Munetaka]], [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏|12=draft}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼|12=draft}} | siblings = Sukeyoshi, {{ill|Muto Yorishige|ja|武藤頼茂|12=draft}}, {{ill|Muto Tameyori|ja|武藤為頼|12=draft}}, {{ill|Muto Yorimoto|ja|武藤頼基|12=draft}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shōni Kagesue|ja|少弐景資|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shōni Morisue|ja|少弐盛資|12=draft}}<ref name="sonpibunmyaku">『尊卑分脈』第2篇・391頁。</ref>, {{ill|Shōni Munetsune|ja|少弐宗資|12=draft}}? | notable = }}
'''Shōni Sukeyoshi''' (少弐 資能) was a [[samurai]] and retainer of the [[Kamakura shogunate]] active during the early to mid-[[Kamakura period]]. He was the second head of the {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏|12=draft}}.
== Biography ==
During Sukeyoshi’s time, the {{ill|Muto clan|ja|武藤氏|12=draft}} held the position of [[Dazai Shōni]], a role associated with governance in the {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|大宰府|12=draft}} region. This responsibility led to the family adopting the surname {{ill|Shōni|ja|少弐氏|12=draft}} starting from Sukeyoshi’s generation.
Sukeyoshi wielded significant power in northern Kyushu and served as the shogunate's {{ill|Chinzei Bugyō|ja|鎮西奉行|12=draft}}. In [[Bun'ei]] 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the [[Yuan dynasty]].<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇|12=draft}} (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資|12=draft}}. Despite his age, Sukeyoshi personally led the Kyushu defense forces and successfully repelled the invading Yuan forces.
In [[Kōan]] 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the {{ill|Second Mongol invasion of Japan|ja|弘安の役|12=draft}}. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い|12=draft}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明|12=draft}} at {{ill|Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)|ja|崇福寺|12=draft}}, {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府|12=draft}}.
In [[Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the {{ill|Shōten-ji|ja|承天寺|12=draft}} in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多|12=draft}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼|12=draft}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989th year (p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺|12=draft}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡|12=draft}} in {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府市|12=draft}}, the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a {{ill|Hōkyōintō|ja|宝篋印塔|1=Hōkyōintō|1=Hōkyōintō|12=draft}} memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>
== References == <references />
== Sources == * 『尊卑分脈』 * 『亀山天皇紀』 * 『後宇多天皇紀』 * 渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989th year

{{Infobox samurai | name = Shōni Sukeyoshi (少弐 資能) | image = | imagesize = | caption = | era = Early to mid-[[Kamakura period]] | birth_date = [[Kenkyū]] 9 (1198) | death_date = [[Kōan]] 4, intercalary [[7th month]], [[13th day (lunar calendar)|13th day]] (August 28, 1281) | posthumous_name = Kakue (覚恵; Dharma name) | alias = Muto Sukeyoshi | rank = [[Dazai Shōni|Assistant Governor of Dazaifu]] | allegiance = [[Kamakura shogunate]] | lord = [[Fujiwara no Yoritsune|Yoritsune]], [[Fujiwara no Yoritsugu|Yoritsugu]], [[Prince Munetaka]], [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏|12=draft}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼|12=draft}} | siblings = Sukeyoshi, {{ill|Muto Yorishige|ja|武藤頼茂|12=draft}}, {{ill|Muto Tameyori|ja|武藤為頼|12=draft}}, {{ill|Muto Yorimoto|ja|武藤頼基|12=draft}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shōni Kagesue|ja|少弐景資|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shōni Morisue|ja|少弐盛資|12=draft}}<ref name="sonpibunmyaku">『尊卑分脈』第2篇・391頁。</ref>, {{ill|Shōni Munetsune|ja|少弐宗資|12=draft}}? | notable = }}
'''Shōni Sukeyoshi''' (少弐 資能) was a [[samurai]] and retainer of the [[Kamakura shogunate]] active during the early to mid-[[Kamakura period]]. He was the second head of the {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏|12=draft}}.
== Biography ==
During Sukeyoshi’s time, the {{ill|Muto clan|ja|武藤氏|12=draft}} held the position of [[Dazai Shōni]], a role associated with governance in the {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|大宰府|12=draft}} region. This responsibility led to the family adopting the surname {{ill|Shōni|ja|少弐氏|12=draft}} starting from Sukeyoshi’s generation.
Sukeyoshi wielded significant power in northern Kyushu and served as the shogunate's {{ill|Chinzei Bugyō|ja|鎮西奉行|12=draft}}. In [[Bun'ei]] 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the [[Yuan dynasty]].<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇|12=draft}} (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資|12=draft}}. Despite his age, Sukeyoshi personally led the Kyushu defense forces and successfully repelled the invading Yuan forces.
In [[Kōan]] 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the {{ill|Second Mongol invasion of Japan|ja|弘安の役|12=draft}}. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い|12=draft}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明|12=draft}} at {{ill|Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)|ja|崇福寺|12=draft}}, {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府|12=draft}}.
In [[Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the {{ill|Shōten-ji|ja|承天寺|12=draft}} in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多|12=draft}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼|12=draft}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989th year (p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺|12=draft}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡|12=draft}} in {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|太宰府市|12=draft}}, the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a {{ill|Hōkyōintō|ja|宝篋印塔|1=Hōkyōintō|2=ja|3=宝篋印塔|4=fr|5=Hōkyōintō|6=nl|7=Hōkyōintō|1=Hōkyōintō|1=Hōkyōintō|12=draft}} memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>
== References == <references />
== Sources == * 『尊卑分脈』 * 『亀山天皇紀』 * 『後宇多天皇紀』 * 渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989th year

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 武士 | 氏名 = 少弐 資能 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[鎌倉時代]]前期 - 中期 | 生誕 = [[建久]]9年（[[1198年]]） | 死没 = [[弘安]]4年閏[[7月13日 (旧暦)|7月13日]]（[[1281年]][[8月28日]]） | 改名 = 資能→覚恵（法号） | 別名 = 武藤資能 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 霊名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[大宰府|大宰少弐]] | 幕府 = [[鎌倉幕府]] | 主君 = [[藤原頼経]]→[[藤原頼嗣|頼嗣]]→[[宗尊親王]]→[[惟康親王]] | 藩 = | 氏族 = [[少弐氏]] | 父母 = 父：[[武藤資頼]] | 兄弟 = '''資能'''、[[武藤頼茂]]、[[武藤為頼]]、[[武藤頼基]] | 妻 = | 子 = [[少弐経資|経資]]、[[少弐景資|景資]]、[[少弐盛資|盛資]]<ref name="sonpibunmyaku">『尊卑分脈』第2篇・391頁。</ref>、[[少弐宗資|宗資]]? | 特記事項 = }}
'''少弐 資能'''（しょうに すけよし）は、[[鎌倉時代]]前期から中期にかけての[[武将]]・[[御家人]]。[[少弐氏]]2代当主。
== 略歴 == 当時[[武藤氏]]は[[大宰府]]の責任者として[[大宰府|大宰少弐]]の地位にあったため、資能の代から[[少弐氏|少弐]]姓を名乗るようになったという。
資能は北九州に大きな勢力を持ち、幕府の[[鎮西奉行]]として、[[文永]]5年（[[1268年]]）、[[元 (王朝)|元]]の使者の対応にも当たっている<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref>。[[元寇|文永の役]]直前に出家して覚恵と号し家督を長男の[[少弐経資|経資]]に譲ったが、老齢の身でありながら自身も九州の総司令官として参戦し、元軍の侵攻を阻止し撃退することに成功する。
[[弘安]]4年（[[1281年]]）の[[元寇|弘安の役]]にも老齢の身を押して出陣した。[[元寇|壱岐島の戦い]]では元軍を相手に奮戦し、壱岐島から元軍を駆逐した<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref>。しかし、この時の負傷がもとでまもなく死去したという。享年84。大応国師[[南浦紹明]]<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年(p.68)</ref>は、導師として[[太宰府]]横岳[[崇福寺 (福岡市)|崇福寺]]において葬儀を執り行った。
なお、[[仁治]]3年（[[1242年]]）に、[[円爾]]（聖一国師）が[[博多]]に[[承天寺]]<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年(p.12)の解釈によると、父の武藤資頼の生前の約束であったのか、没後14年を経ているにもかかわらず、承天寺の喜捨(捨地壇越)は父資頼として、その名をたたえているのである。</ref> を開く際に、資能は寺地数万坪を寄進した。
[[太宰府市]]の[[観世音寺]]の北にある観世音寺四十九子院跡<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html に九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>のひとつといわれる安養寺の跡地の一角には、[[武藤資頼]]墓と伝えられる五輪塔と少弐資能の供養塔(宝篋印塔)が並んで建っている。
== 脚注 == <references />
== 出典 == *『[[尊卑分脈]]』 *『亀山天皇紀』 *『後宇多天皇紀』 *渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989年

'''Shōni Sukeyoshi''' (少弐 資能) was a [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]] and retainer of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]] active during the early to mid-[[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. He was the second head of the [[:en:Shōni clan|Shōni clan]].
== Biography ==
During Sukeyoshi’s time, the {{ill|Muto clan|ja|武藤氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muto clan|qq=|1=Mutō clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546509}} held the position of [[Dazai Shōni]], a role associated with governance in the [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] region. This responsibility led to the family adopting the surname [[:en:Shōni clan|Shōni]] starting from Sukeyoshi’s generation.

In [[:en:Bun'ei|Bun'ei]] 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the [[:en:Yuan dynasty|Yuan dynasty]].<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]] (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kyonori|qq=|1=Shōni Tsunesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464740}}. Despite his age, Sukeyoshi personally led the Kyushu defense forces and successfully repelled the invading Yuan forces.
In [[:en:Koan|Koan]] 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Second Mongol invasion of Japan]]. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki Island|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daionkokushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at [[:en:Sōfuku-ji|Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)]], [[:en:Dazaifu|Dazaifu]].
In [[:en:Ninji|Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the [[:en:Jōten-ji|Shōten-ji]] in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata|qq=|1=Hakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11284628}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muto Sukeyori|qq=|1=Muto Sukeyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546530}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989th year (p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anyo-ji|qq=|1=Anyo-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451679}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon-ji Temple|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]], the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a [[:en:Hōkyōintō|Hōkyōintō]] memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>

'''Shōni Sukeyoshi''' (少弐 資能) was a [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]] and retainer of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]] active during the early to mid-[[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. He was the second head of the [[:en:Shōni clan|Shōni clan]].
== Biography ==
During Sukeyoshi’s time, the {{ill|Muto clan|ja|武藤氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muto clan|qq=|1=Mutō clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546509}} held the position of [[Dazai Shōni]], a role associated with governance in the [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] region. This responsibility led to the family adopting the surname [[:en:Shōni clan|Shōni]] starting from Sukeyoshi’s generation.
Sukeyoshi wielded significant power in northern Kyushu and served as the shogunate's [[:en:Chinzei Bugyō|Chinzei Bugyō]]. In [[:en:Bun'ei|Bun'ei]] 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the [[:en:Yuan dynasty|Yuan dynasty]].<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]] (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kyonori|qq=|1=Shōni Tsunesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464740}}. Despite his age, Sukeyoshi personally led the Kyushu defense forces and successfully repelled the invading Yuan forces.
In [[:en:Koan|Koan]] 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Second Mongol invasion of Japan]]. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki Island|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daionkokushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at [[:en:Sōfuku-ji|Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)]], [[:en:Dazaifu|Dazaifu]].
In [[:en:Ninji|Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the [[:en:Jōten-ji|Shōten-ji]] in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata|qq=|1=Hakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11284628}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muto Sukeyori|qq=|1=Muto Sukeyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546530}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989th year (p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anyo-ji|qq=|1=Anyo-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451679}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon-ji Temple|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]], the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a [[:en:Hōkyōintō|Hōkyōintō]] memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>

'''Shōni Sukeyoshi''' (少弐 資能) was a [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]] and retainer of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]] active during the early to mid-[[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. He was the second head of the [[:en:Shōni clan|Shōni clan]].
== Biography ==
During Sukeyoshi’s time, the {{ill|Muto clan|ja|武藤氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muto clan|1=Mutō clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546509}} held the position of [[Dazai Shōni]], a role associated with governance in the [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] region. This responsibility led to the family adopting the surname [[:en:Shōni clan|Shōni]] starting from Sukeyoshi’s generation.
Sukeyoshi wielded significant power in northern Kyushu and served as the shogunate's [[:en:Chinzei Bugyō|Chinzei Bugyō]]. In [[:en:Bun'ei|Bun'ei]] 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the [[:en:Yuan dynasty|Yuan dynasty]].<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]] (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kyonori|1=Shōni Tsunesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464740}}. Despite his age, Sukeyoshi personally led the Kyushu defense forces and successfully repelled the invading Yuan forces.
In [[:en:Koan|Koan]] 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Second Mongol invasion of Japan]]. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki Island|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daionkokushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at [[:en:Sōfuku-ji|Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)]], [[:en:Dazaifu|Dazaifu]].
In [[:en:Ninji|Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the [[:en:Jōten-ji|Shōten-ji]] in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata|1=Hakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11284628}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muto Sukeyori|1=Muto Sukeyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546530}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989th year (p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anyo-ji|1=Anyo-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451679}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon-ji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]], the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a [[:en:Hōkyōintō|Hōkyōintō]] memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>

Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anyo-ji|1=Anyo-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451679}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon-ji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]], the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a [[:en:Hōkyōintō|Hōkyōintō]] memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>

'''Shōni Sukeyoshi''' (少弐 資能) was a [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]] and retainer of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]] active during the early to mid-[[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. He was the second head of the [[:en:Shōni clan|Shōni clan]].
== Biography ==
During Sukeyoshi’s time, the {{ill|Muto clan|ja|武藤氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muto clan|1=Mutō clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546509}} held the position of [[Dazai Shōni]], a role associated with governance in the [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] region. This responsibility led to the family adopting the surname [[:en:Shōni clan|Shōni]] starting from Sukeyoshi’s generation.
Sukeyoshi wielded significant power in northern Kyushu and served as the shogunate's [[:en:Chinzei Bugyō|Chinzei Bugyō]]. In [[:en:Bun'ei|Bun'ei]] 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the [[:en:Yuan dynasty|Yuan dynasty]].<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]] (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kyonori|1=Shōni Tsunesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464740}}. Despite his age, Sukeyoshi personally led the Kyushu defense forces and successfully repelled the invading Yuan forces.
In [[:en:Koan|Koan]] 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Second Mongol invasion of Japan]]. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki Island|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daionkokushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at [[:en:Sōfuku-ji|Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)]], [[:en:Dazaifu|Dazaifu]].
In [[:en:Ninji|Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the [[:en:Jōten-ji|Shōten-ji]] in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata|1=Hakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11284628}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muto Sukeyori|1=Muto Sukeyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546530}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989th year (p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anyo-ji|1=Anyo-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451679}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon-ji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]], the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a [[:en:Hōkyōintō|Hōkyōintō]] memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>

This responsibility led to the family adopting the surname [[:en:Shōni clan|Shōni]] starting from Sukeyoshi’s generation.
Sukeyoshi wielded significant power in northern Kyushu and served as the shogunate's [[:en:Chinzei Bugyō|Chinzei Bugyō]]. In [[:en:Bun'ei|Bun'ei]] 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the [[:en:Yuan dynasty|Yuan dynasty]].<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]] (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kyonori|1=Shōni Tsunesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464740}}. Despite his age, Sukeyoshi personally led the Kyushu defense forces and successfully repelled the invading Yuan forces.
In [[:en:Koan|Koan]] 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Second Mongol invasion of Japan]]. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki Island|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daionkokushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at [[:en:Sōfuku-ji|Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)]], [[:en:Dazaifu|Dazaifu]].
In [[:en:Ninji|Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the [[:en:Jōten-ji|Shōten-ji]] in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata|1=Hakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11284628}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muto Sukeyori|1=Muto Sukeyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546530}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989th year (p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anyo-ji|1=Anyo-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451679}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon-ji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]], the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a [[:en:Hōkyōintō|Hōkyōintō]] memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>

'''Shōni Sukeyoshi''' (少弐 資能) was a [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]] and retainer of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]] active during the early to mid-[[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. He was the second head of the [[:en:Shōni clan|Shōni clan]].
== Biography ==
During Sukeyoshi’s time, the {{ill|Muto clan|ja|武藤氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muto clan|1=Mutō clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546509}} held the position of [[Dazai Shōni]], a role associated with governance in the [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] region. This responsibility led to the family adopting the surname [[:en:Shōni clan|Shōni]] starting from Sukeyoshi’s generation.
Sukeyoshi wielded significant power in northern Kyushu and served as the shogunate's [[:en:Chinzei Bugyō|Chinzei Bugyō]]. In [[:en:Bun'ei|Bun'ei]] 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the [[:en:Yuan dynasty|Yuan dynasty]].<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]] (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kyonori|1=Shōni Tsunesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464740}}. Despite his age, Sukeyoshi personally led the Kyushu defense forces and successfully repelled the invading Yuan forces.
In [[:en:Koan|Koan]] 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Second Mongol invasion of Japan]]. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki Island|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daionkokushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at [[:en:Sōfuku-ji|Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)]], [[:en:Dazaifu|Dazaifu]].
In [[:en:Ninji|Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the [[:en:Jōten-ji|Shōten-ji]] in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata|1=Hakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11284628}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muto Sukeyori|1=Muto Sukeyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546530}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989th year (p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anyo-ji|1=Anyo-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451679}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon-ji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]], the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a [[:en:Hōkyōintō|Hōkyōintō]] memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>
== References == <references />
== Sources == * 『尊卑分脈』 * 『亀山天皇紀』 * 『後宇多天皇紀』 * 渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989th year

Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anyo-ji|1=Anyo-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451679}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon-ji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]], the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a [[:en:Hōkyōintō|Hōkyōintō]] memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>

'''Shōni Sukeyoshi''' (少弐 資能) was a [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]] and retainer of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]] active during the early to mid-[[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. He was the second head of the [[:en:Shōni clan|Shōni clan]].
== Biography ==
During Sukeyoshi’s time, the {{ill|Muto clan|ja|武藤氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muto clan|1=Mutō clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546509}} held the position of [[Dazai Shōni]], a role associated with governance in the [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] region. This responsibility led to the family adopting the surname [[:en:Shōni clan|Shōni]] starting from Sukeyoshi’s generation.
Sukeyoshi wielded significant power in northern Kyushu and served as the shogunate's [[:en:Chinzei Bugyō|Chinzei Bugyō]]. In [[:en:Bun'ei|Bun'ei]] 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the [[:en:Yuan dynasty|Yuan dynasty]].<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]] (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kyonori|1=Shōni Tsunesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464740}}. Despite his age, Sukeyoshi personally led the Kyushu defense forces and successfully repelled the invading Yuan forces.
In [[:en:Koan|Koan]] 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Second Mongol invasion of Japan]]. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki Island|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daionkokushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at [[:en:Sōfuku-ji|Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)]], [[:en:Dazaifu|Dazaifu]].
In [[:en:Ninji|Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the [[:en:Jōten-ji|Shōten-ji]] in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata|1=Hakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11284628}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muto Sukeyori|1=Muto Sukeyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546530}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989th year (p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anyo-ji|1=Anyo-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451679}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon-ji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]], the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a [[:en:Hōkyōintō|Hōkyōintō]] memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>

'''Shōni Sukeyoshi''' (少弐 資能) was a [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]] and retainer of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]] active during the early to mid-[[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. He was the second head of the [[:en:Shōni clan|Shōni clan]].
== Biography ==
During Sukeyoshi’s time, the {{ill|Muto clan|ja|武藤氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muto clan|1=Mutō clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546509}} held the position of [[Dazai Shōni]], a role associated with governance in the [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] region. This responsibility led to the family adopting the surname [[:en:Shōni clan|Shōni]] starting from Sukeyoshi’s generation.
Sukeyoshi wielded significant power in northern Kyushu and served as the shogunate's [[:en:Chinzei Bugyō|Chinzei Bugyō]]. In [[:en:Bun'ei|Bun'ei]] 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the [[:en:Yuan dynasty|Yuan dynasty]].<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]] (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kyonori|1=Shōni Tsunesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464740}}. Despite his age, Sukeyoshi personally led the Kyushu defense forces and successfully repelled the invading Yuan forces.
In [[:en:Koan|Koan]] 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Second Mongol invasion of Japan]]. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki Island|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daionkokushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at [[:en:Sōfuku-ji|Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)]], [[:en:Dazaifu|Dazaifu]].
In [[:en:Ninji|Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the [[:en:Jōten-ji|Shōten-ji]] in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata|1=Hakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11284628}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muto Sukeyori|1=Muto Sukeyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546530}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989th year (p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anyo-ji|1=Anyo-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451679}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon-ji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]], the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a [[:en:Hōkyōintō|Hōkyōintō]] memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>
== References == <references />
== Sources == * 『尊卑分脈』 * 『亀山天皇紀』 * 『後宇多天皇紀』 * 渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989th year

'''Shōni Sukeyoshi''' (少弐 資能) was a [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]] and retainer of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]] active during the early to mid-[[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. He was the second head of the [[:en:Shōni clan|Shōni clan]].
== Biography ==
During Sukeyoshi’s time, the {{ill|Muto clan|ja|武藤氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muto clan|1=Mutō clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546509}} held the position of [[Dazai Shōni]], a role associated with governance in the [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] region. This responsibility led to the family adopting the surname [[:en:Shōni clan|Shōni]] starting from Sukeyoshi’s generation.
Sukeyoshi wielded significant power in northern Kyushu and served as the shogunate's [[:en:Chinzei Bugyō|Chinzei Bugyō]]. In [[:en:Bun'ei|Bun'ei]] 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the [[:en:Yuan dynasty|Yuan dynasty]].<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]] (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kyonori|1=Shōni Tsunesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464740}}. Despite his age, Sukeyoshi personally led the Kyushu defense forces and successfully repelled the invading Yuan forces.
In [[:en:Koan|Koan]] 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Second Mongol invasion of Japan]]. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki Island|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daionkokushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at [[:en:Sōfuku-ji|Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)]], [[:en:Dazaifu|Dazaifu]].
In [[:en:Ninji|Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the [[:en:Jōten-ji|Shōten-ji]] in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata|1=Hakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11284628}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muto Sukeyori|1=Muto Sukeyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546530}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989th year (p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anyo-ji|1=Anyo-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451679}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon-ji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]], the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a [[:en:Hōkyōintō|Hōkyōintō]] memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>

This responsibility led to the family adopting the surname [[:en:Shōni clan|Shōni]] starting from Sukeyoshi’s generation.
Sukeyoshi wielded significant power in northern Kyushu and served as the shogunate's [[:en:Chinzei Bugyō|Chinzei Bugyō]]. In [[:en:Bun'ei|Bun'ei]] 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the [[:en:Yuan dynasty|Yuan dynasty]].<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]] (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kyonori|1=Shōni Tsunesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464740}}. Despite his age, Sukeyoshi personally led the Kyushu defense forces and successfully repelled the invading Yuan forces.
In [[:en:Koan|Koan]] 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Second Mongol invasion of Japan]]. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki Island|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daionkokushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at [[:en:Sōfuku-ji|Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)]], [[:en:Dazaifu|Dazaifu]].
In [[:en:Ninji|Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the [[:en:Jōten-ji|Shōten-ji]] in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata|1=Hakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11284628}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muto Sukeyori|1=Muto Sukeyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546530}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989th year (p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anyo-ji|1=Anyo-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451679}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon-ji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]], the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a [[:en:Hōkyōintō|Hōkyōintō]] memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>

'''Shōni Sukeyoshi''' (少弐 資能) was a [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]] and retainer of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]] active during the early to mid-[[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. He was the second head of the [[:en:Shōni clan|Shōni clan]].
== Biography ==
During Sukeyoshi’s time, the {{ill|Muto clan|ja|武藤氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muto clan|1=Mutō clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546509}} held the position of [[Dazai Shōni]], a role associated with governance in the [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] region. This responsibility led to the family adopting the surname [[:en:Shōni clan|Shōni]] starting from Sukeyoshi’s generation.
Sukeyoshi wielded significant power in northern Kyushu and served as the shogunate's [[:en:Chinzei Bugyō|Chinzei Bugyō]]. In [[:en:Bun'ei|Bun'ei]] 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the [[:en:Yuan dynasty|Yuan dynasty]].<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]] (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kyonori|1=Shōni Tsunesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464740}}. Despite his age, Sukeyoshi personally led the Kyushu defense forces and successfully repelled the invading Yuan forces.
In [[:en:Koan|Koan]] 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Second Mongol invasion of Japan]]. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki Island|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daionkokushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at [[:en:Sōfuku-ji|Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)]], [[:en:Dazaifu|Dazaifu]].
In [[:en:Ninji|Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the [[:en:Jōten-ji|Shōten-ji]] in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata|1=Hakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11284628}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muto Sukeyori|1=Muto Sukeyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546530}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989th year (p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anyo-ji|1=Anyo-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451679}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon-ji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]], the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a [[:en:Hōkyōintō|Hōkyōintō]] memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>
== References == <references />

In [[:en:Ninji|Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the [[:en:Jōten-ji|Shōten-ji]] in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata|1=Hakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11284628}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muto Sukeyori|1=Muto Sukeyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546530}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989th year (p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anyo-ji|1=Anyo-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451679}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon-ji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]], the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a [[:en:Hōkyōintō|Hōkyōintō]] memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>

'''Shōni Sukeyoshi''' (少弐 資能) was a [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]] and retainer of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]] active during the early to mid-[[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. He was the second head of the [[:en:Shōni clan|Shōni clan]].
== Biography ==
During Sukeyoshi’s time, the {{ill|Muto clan|ja|武藤氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muto clan|1=Mutō clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546509}} held the position of [[Dazai Shōni]], a role associated with governance in the [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] region. This responsibility led to the family adopting the surname [[:en:Shōni clan|Shōni]] starting from Sukeyoshi’s generation.
Sukeyoshi wielded significant power in northern Kyushu and served as the shogunate's [[:en:Chinzei Bugyō|Chinzei Bugyō]]. In [[:en:Bun'ei|Bun'ei]] 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the [[:en:Yuan dynasty|Yuan dynasty]].<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]] (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kyonori|1=Shōni Tsunesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464740}}. Despite his age, Sukeyoshi personally led the Kyushu defense forces and successfully repelled the invading Yuan forces.
In [[:en:Koan|Koan]] 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Second Mongol invasion of Japan]]. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki Island|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daionkokushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at [[:en:Sōfuku-ji|Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)]], [[:en:Dazaifu|Dazaifu]].
In [[:en:Ninji|Ninji]] 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the [[:en:Jōten-ji|Shōten-ji]] in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata|1=Hakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11284628}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muto Sukeyori|1=Muto Sukeyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546530}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989th year (p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anyo-ji|1=Anyo-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451679}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon-ji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]], the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a [[:en:Hōkyōintō|Hōkyōintō]] memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>

'''Shōni Sukeyoshi''' (少弐 資能) was a {{ill|Samurai|en|Samurai|WD=Q38142|lt=Samurai}} and retainer of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|WD=Q736839|lt=Kamakura shogunate}} active during the early to mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}. He was the second head of the {{ill|Shōni clan|en|Shōni clan|WD=Q2975368|lt=Shōni clan}}.
== Biography ==
During Sukeyoshi’s time, the {{ill|Muto clan|ja|武藤氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muto clan|1=Mutō clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546509}} held the position of [[Dazai Shōni]], a role associated with governance in the {{ill|Dazaifu (government)|en|Dazaifu (government)|WD=Q144845|lt=Dazaifu}} region. This responsibility led to the family adopting the surname {{ill|Shōni clan|en|Shōni clan|WD=Q2975368|lt=Shōni}} starting from Sukeyoshi’s generation.
Sukeyoshi wielded significant power in northern Kyushu and served as the shogunate's {{ill|Chinzei Bugyō|en|Chinzei Bugyō|WD=Q440978|lt=Chinzei Bugyō}}. In {{ill|Bun'ei|en|Bun'ei|WD=Q1010103|lt=Bun'ei}} 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the {{ill|Yuan dynasty|en|Yuan dynasty|WD=Q7313|lt=Yuan dynasty}}.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|en|Mongol invasions of Japan|WD=Q208156|lt=Mongol invasions of Japan}} (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kyonori|1=Shōni Tsunesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464740}}. Despite his age, Sukeyoshi personally led the Kyushu defense forces and successfully repelled the invading Yuan forces.
In {{ill|Koan|en|Koan|WD=Q1231764|lt=Koan}} 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the {{ill|Battle of Kōan|en|Battle of Kōan|WD=Q2237622|lt=Second Mongol invasion of Japan}}. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki Island|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daionkokushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at {{ill|Sōfuku-ji|en|Sōfuku-ji|WD=Q1062452|lt=Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)}}, {{ill|Dazaifu|en|Dazaifu|WD=Q1179277|lt=Dazaifu}}.
In {{ill|Ninji|en|Ninji|WD=Q1194262|lt=Ninji}} 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the {{ill|Jōten-ji|en|Jōten-ji|WD=Q3191242|lt=Shōten-ji}} in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata|1=Hakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11284628}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muto Sukeyori|1=Muto Sukeyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546530}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989th year (p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anyo-ji|1=Anyo-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451679}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon-ji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Dazaifu, Fukuoka|en|Dazaifu, Fukuoka|WD=Q728218|lt=Dazaifu}}, the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a {{ill|Hōkyōintō|en|Hōkyōintō|WD=Q3146365|lt=Hōkyōintō}} memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>

{{Infobox samurai | name = Shōni Sukeyoshi (少弐 資能) | image = | imagesize = | caption = | era = Early to mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}} | birth_date = [[Kenkyū]] 9 (1198) | death_date = {{ill|Koan|en|Koan|WD=Q1231764|lt=Koan}} 4, intercalary [[7th month]], [[13th day (lunar calendar)|13th day]] (August 28, 1281) | posthumous_name = Kakue (覚恵; Dharma name) | alias = Muto Sukeyoshi | Rank = [[Dazai Shōni|Assistant Governor of Dazaifu]] | allegiance = {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|WD=Q736839|lt=Kamakura shogunate}} | lord = [[Fujiwara no Yoritsune|Yoritsune]], [[Fujiwara no Yoritsugu|Yoritsugu]], [[Prince Munetaka]], [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = {{ill|Shōni clan|en|Shōni clan|WD=Q2975368|lt=Shōni clan}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muto Sukeyori|1=Muto Sukeyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546530}} | siblings = Sukeyoshi, {{ill|Muto Yorishige|ja|武藤頼茂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muto Yorishige|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Muto Tameyori|ja|武藤為頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muto Tameyori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Muto Yorimoto|ja|武藤頼基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muto Yorimoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kyonori|1=Shōni Tsunesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464740}}, {{ill|Shōni Kagesue|ja|少弐景資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesue|1=Shōni Kagesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10263912}}, {{ill|Shōni Morisue|ja|少弐盛資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Morisue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="sonpibunmyaku">『尊卑分脈』第2篇・391頁。</ref>, {{ill|Shōni Munetsune|ja|少弐宗資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Munetsune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}? | notable = }}
'''Shōni Sukeyoshi''' (少弐 資能) was a {{ill|Samurai|en|Samurai|WD=Q38142|lt=Samurai}} and retainer of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|WD=Q736839|lt=Kamakura shogunate}} active during the early to mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}. He was the second head of the {{ill|Shōni clan|en|Shōni clan|WD=Q2975368|lt=Shōni clan}}.
== Biography ==
During Sukeyoshi’s time, the {{ill|Muto clan|ja|武藤氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muto clan|1=Mutō clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546509}} held the position of [[Dazai Shōni]], a role associated with governance in the {{ill|Dazaifu (government)|en|Dazaifu (government)|WD=Q144845|lt=Dazaifu}} region. This responsibility led to the family adopting the surname {{ill|Shōni clan|en|Shōni clan|WD=Q2975368|lt=Shōni}} starting from Sukeyoshi’s generation.
Sukeyoshi wielded significant power in northern Kyushu and served as the shogunate's {{ill|Chinzei Bugyō|en|Chinzei Bugyō|WD=Q440978|lt=Chinzei Bugyō}}. In {{ill|Bun'ei|en|Bun'ei|WD=Q1010103|lt=Bun'ei}} 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the {{ill|Yuan dynasty|en|Yuan dynasty|WD=Q7313|lt=Yuan dynasty}}.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|en|Mongol invasions of Japan|WD=Q208156|lt=Mongol invasions of Japan}} (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kyonori|1=Shōni Tsunesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464740}}. Despite his age, Sukeyoshi personally led the Kyushu defense forces and successfully repelled the invading Yuan forces.
In {{ill|Koan|en|Koan|WD=Q1231764|lt=Koan}} 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the {{ill|Battle of Kōan|en|Battle of Kōan|WD=Q2237622|lt=Second Mongol invasion of Japan}}. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki Island|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daionkokushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at {{ill|Sōfuku-ji|en|Sōfuku-ji|WD=Q1062452|lt=Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)}}, {{ill|Dazaifu|en|Dazaifu|WD=Q1179277|lt=Dazaifu}}.
In {{ill|Ninji|en|Ninji|WD=Q1194262|lt=Ninji}} 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the {{ill|Jōten-ji|en|Jōten-ji|WD=Q3191242|lt=Shōten-ji}} in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata|1=Hakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11284628}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muto Sukeyori|1=Muto Sukeyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546530}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989th year (p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anyo-ji|1=Anyo-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451679}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon-ji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Dazaifu, Fukuoka|en|Dazaifu, Fukuoka|WD=Q728218|lt=Dazaifu}}, the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a {{ill|Hōkyōintō|en|Hōkyōintō|WD=Q3146365|lt=Hōkyōintō}} memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>
== References == <references />
== Sources == * 『尊卑分脈』 * 『亀山天皇紀』 * 『後宇多天皇紀』 * 渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989th year

'''Shōni Sukeyoshi''' (少弐 資能) was a {{ill|Samurai|en|Samurai|WD=Q38142|lt=Samurai}} and retainer of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|WD=Q736839|lt=Kamakura shogunate}} active during the early to mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}. During Sukeyoshi’s time, the {{ill|Muto clan|ja|武藤氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muto clan|1=Mutō clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546509}} held the position of [[Dazai Shōni]], a role associated with governance in the {{ill|Dazaifu (government)|en|Dazaifu (government)|WD=Q144845|lt=Dazaifu}} region. Sukeyoshi wielded significant power in northern Kyushu and served as the shogunate's {{ill|Chinzei Bugyō|en|Chinzei Bugyō|WD=Q440978|lt=Chinzei Bugyō}}. In {{ill|Bun'ei|en|Bun'ei|WD=Q1010103|lt=Bun'ei}} 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the {{ill|Yuan dynasty|en|Yuan dynasty|WD=Q7313|lt=Yuan dynasty}}.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|en|Mongol invasions of Japan|WD=Q208156|lt=Mongol invasions of Japan}} (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kyonori|1=Shōni Tsunesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464740}}. In {{ill|Bun'ei|en|Bun'ei|WD=Q1010103|lt=Bun'ei}} 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the {{ill|Yuan dynasty|en|Yuan dynasty|WD=Q7313|lt=Yuan dynasty}}.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|en|Mongol invasions of Japan|WD=Q208156|lt=Mongol invasions of Japan}} (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kyonori|1=Shōni Tsunesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464740}}. Despite his age, Sukeyoshi personally led the Kyushu defense forces and successfully repelled the invading Yuan forces.
In {{ill|Koan|en|Koan|WD=Q1231764|lt=Koan}} 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the {{ill|Battle of Kōan|en|Battle of Kōan|WD=Q2237622|lt=Second Mongol invasion of Japan}}. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki Island|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daionkokushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at {{ill|Sōfuku-ji|en|Sōfuku-ji|WD=Q1062452|lt=Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)}}, {{ill|Dazaifu|en|Dazaifu|WD=Q1179277|lt=Dazaifu}}.
In {{ill|Ninji|en|Ninji|WD=Q1194262|lt=Ninji}} 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the {{ill|Jōten-ji|en|Jōten-ji|WD=Q3191242|lt=Shōten-ji}} in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata|1=Hakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11284628}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muto Sukeyori|1=Muto Sukeyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546530}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989th year (p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anyo-ji|1=Anyo-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451679}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon-ji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Dazaifu, Fukuoka|en|Dazaifu, Fukuoka|WD=Q728218|lt=Dazaifu}}, the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a {{ill|Hōkyōintō|en|Hōkyōintō|WD=Q3146365|lt=Hōkyōintō}} memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>

'''Shōni Sukeyoshi''' (少弐 資能) was a {{ill|Samurai|en|Samurai|WD=Q38142|lt=Samurai}} and retainer of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|WD=Q736839|lt=Kamakura shogunate}} active during the early to mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}. He was the second head of the {{ill|Shōni clan|en|Shōni clan|WD=Q2975368|lt=Shōni clan}}.
== Biography ==
During Sukeyoshi’s time, the {{ill|Muto clan|ja|武藤氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muto clan|1=Mutō clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546509}} held the position of [[Dazai Shōni]], a role associated with governance in the {{ill|Dazaifu (government)|en|Dazaifu (government)|WD=Q144845|lt=Dazaifu}} region. This responsibility led to the family adopting the surname {{ill|Shōni clan|en|Shōni clan|WD=Q2975368|lt=Shōni}} starting from Sukeyoshi’s generation.
Sukeyoshi wielded significant power in northern Kyushu and served as the shogunate's {{ill|Chinzei Bugyō|en|Chinzei Bugyō|WD=Q440978|lt=Chinzei Bugyō}}. In {{ill|Bun'ei|en|Bun'ei|WD=Q1010103|lt=Bun'ei}} 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the {{ill|Yuan dynasty|en|Yuan dynasty|WD=Q7313|lt=Yuan dynasty}}.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|en|Mongol invasions of Japan|WD=Q208156|lt=Mongol invasions of Japan}} (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kyonori|1=Shōni Tsunesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464740}}. Despite his age, Sukeyoshi personally led the Kyushu defense forces and successfully repelled the invading Yuan forces.
In {{ill|Koan|en|Koan|WD=Q1231764|lt=Koan}} 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the {{ill|Battle of Kōan|en|Battle of Kōan|WD=Q2237622|lt=Second Mongol invasion of Japan}}. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki Island|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daionkokushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at {{ill|Sōfuku-ji|en|Sōfuku-ji|WD=Q1062452|lt=Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)}}, {{ill|Dazaifu|en|Dazaifu|WD=Q1179277|lt=Dazaifu}}.
In {{ill|Ninji|en|Ninji|WD=Q1194262|lt=Ninji}} 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the {{ill|Jōten-ji|en|Jōten-ji|WD=Q3191242|lt=Shōten-ji}} in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata|1=Hakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11284628}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muto Sukeyori|1=Muto Sukeyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546530}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989th year (p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anyo-ji|1=Anyo-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451679}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon-ji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Dazaifu, Fukuoka|en|Dazaifu, Fukuoka|WD=Q728218|lt=Dazaifu}}, the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a {{ill|Hōkyōintō|en|Hōkyōintō|WD=Q3146365|lt=Hōkyōintō}} memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>

{{Infobox samurai | name = Shōni Sukeyoshi (少弐 資能) | image = | imagesize = | caption = | era = Early to mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}} | birth_date = [[Kenkyū]] 9 (1198) | death_date = {{ill|Koan|en|Koan|WD=Q1231764|lt=Koan|1=Koan}} 4, intercalary [[7th month]], [[13th day (lunar calendar)|13th day]] (August 28, 1281) | posthumous_name = Kakue (覚恵; Dharma name) | alias = Muto Sukeyoshi | Rank = [[Dazai Shōni|Assistant Governor of Dazaifu]] | allegiance = {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|WD=Q736839|lt=Kamakura shogunate|1=Kamakura shogunate}} | lord = [[Fujiwara no Yoritsune|Yoritsune]], [[Fujiwara no Yoritsugu|Yoritsugu]], [[Prince Munetaka]], [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = {{ill|Shōni clan|en|Shōni clan|WD=Q2975368|lt=Shōni clan|1=Shōni clan}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muto Sukeyori|1=Q11546530|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546530}} | siblings = Sukeyoshi, {{ill|Muto Yorishige|ja|武藤頼茂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muto Yorishige|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Muto Tameyori|ja|武藤為頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muto Tameyori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Muto Yorimoto|ja|武藤頼基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muto Yorimoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kyonori|1=Shōni Kagesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464740}}, {{ill|Shōni Kagesue|ja|少弐景資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesue|1=Shōni Kagesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10263912}}, {{ill|Shōni Morisue|ja|少弐盛資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Morisue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="sonpibunmyaku">『尊卑分脈』第2篇・391頁。</ref>, {{ill|Shōni Munetsune|ja|少弐宗資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Munetsune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}? | notable = }}
'''Shōni Sukeyoshi''' (少弐 資能) was a {{ill|Samurai|en|Samurai|WD=Q38142|lt=Samurai|1=Samurai}} and retainer of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|WD=Q736839|lt=Kamakura shogunate|1=Kamakura shogunate}} active during the early to mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}. He was the second head of the {{ill|Shōni clan|en|Shōni clan|WD=Q2975368|lt=Shōni clan|1=Shōni clan}}.
== Biography ==
During Sukeyoshi’s time, the {{ill|Muto clan|ja|武藤氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muto clan|1=Mutō clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546509}} held the position of [[Dazai Shōni]], a role associated with governance in the {{ill|Dazaifu (government)|en|Dazaifu (government)|WD=Q144845|lt=Dazaifu|1=Dazaifu (government)}} region. This responsibility led to the family adopting the surname {{ill|Shōni clan|en|Shōni clan|WD=Q2975368|lt=Shōni|1=Shōni clan}} starting from Sukeyoshi’s generation.
Sukeyoshi wielded significant power in northern Kyushu and served as the shogunate's {{ill|Chinzei Bugyō|en|Chinzei Bugyō|WD=Q440978|lt=Chinzei Bugyō|1=Chinzei Bugyō}}. In {{ill|Bun'ei|en|Bun'ei|WD=Q1010103|lt=Bun'ei|1=Bun'ei}} 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the {{ill|Yuan dynasty|en|Yuan dynasty|WD=Q7313|lt=Yuan dynasty|1=Yuan dynasty}}.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|en|Mongol invasions of Japan|WD=Q208156|lt=Mongol invasions of Japan|1=Mongol invasions of Japan}} (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kyonori|1=Shōni Kagesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464740}}. Despite his age, Sukeyoshi personally led the Kyushu defense forces and successfully repelled the invading Yuan forces.
In {{ill|Koan|en|Koan|WD=Q1231764|lt=Koan|1=Koan}} 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the {{ill|Battle of Kōan|en|Battle of Kōan|WD=Q2237622|lt=Second Mongol invasion of Japan|1=Battle of Kōan}}. He fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の戦い|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki Island|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, driving the Yuan forces off Iki Island.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 258頁</ref> However, he succumbed to injuries sustained in the campaign and died shortly afterward at the age of 84. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daionkokushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at {{ill|Sōfuku-ji|en|Sōfuku-ji|WD=Q1062452|lt=Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)|1=Sōfuku-ji}}, {{ill|Dazaifu|en|Dazaifu|WD=Q1179277|lt=Dazaifu|1=Dazaifu}}.
In {{ill|Ninji|en|Ninji|WD=Q1194262|lt=Ninji|1=Ninji}} 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the {{ill|Jōten-ji|en|Jōten-ji|WD=Q3191242|lt=Shōten-ji|1=Jōten-ji}} in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata|1=Hakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11284628}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muto Sukeyori|1=Q11546530|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546530}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989th year (p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anyo-ji|1=Q11451679|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451679}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon-ji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Dazaifu, Fukuoka|en|Dazaifu, Fukuoka|WD=Q728218|lt=Dazaifu|1=Dazaifu, Fukuoka}}, the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a {{ill|Hōkyōintō|en|Hōkyōintō|WD=Q3146365|lt=Hōkyōintō|1=Hōkyōintō}} memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>

'''Shōni Sukeyoshi''' (少弐 資能) was a {{ill|Samurai|en|Samurai|WD=Q38142|lt=Samurai|1=Samurai}} and retainer of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|WD=Q736839|lt=Kamakura shogunate|1=Kamakura shogunate}} active during the early to mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}. He was the second head of the {{ill|Shōni clan|en|Shōni clan|WD=Q2975368|lt=Shōni clan|1=Shōni clan}}.
== Biography ==
During Sukeyoshi’s time, the {{ill|Muto clan|ja|武藤氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muto clan|1=Mutō clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546509}} held the position of [[Dazai Shōni]], a role associated with governance in the {{ill|Dazaifu (government)|en|Dazaifu (government)|WD=Q144845|lt=Dazaifu|1=Dazaifu (government)}} region. This responsibility led to the family adopting the surname {{ill|Shōni clan|en|Shōni clan|WD=Q2975368|lt=Shōni|1=Shōni clan}} starting from Sukeyoshi’s generation.
Sukeyoshi wielded significant power in northern Kyushu and served as the shogunate's {{ill|Chinzei Bugyō|en|Chinzei Bugyō|WD=Q440978|lt=Chinzei Bugyō|1=Chinzei Bugyō}}. In {{ill|Bun'ei|en|Bun'ei|WD=Q1010103|lt=Bun'ei|1=Bun'ei}} 5 (1268), he handled the shogunate’s response to envoys from the {{ill|Yuan dynasty|en|Yuan dynasty|WD=Q7313|lt=Yuan dynasty|1=Yuan dynasty}}.<ref>『史料綜覧』第5編之905 144頁</ref> Shortly before the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|en|Mongol invasions of Japan|WD=Q208156|lt=Mongol invasions of Japan|1=Mongol invasions of Japan}} (Bun'ei Campaign), Sukeyoshi took religious vows, adopting the name Kakue, and passed his leadership to his eldest son, {{ill|Shōni Kyonori|ja|少弐経資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kyonori|1=Shōni Kagesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464740}}. In {{ill|Koan|en|Koan|WD=Q1231764|lt=Koan|1=Koan}} 4 (1281), Sukeyoshi participated in the {{ill|Battle of Kōan|en|Battle of Kōan|WD=Q2237622|lt=Second Mongol invasion of Japan|1=Battle of Kōan}}. His funeral was conducted by {{ill|Daionkokushi|ja|大応国師南浦紹明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daionkokushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at {{ill|Sōfuku-ji|en|Sōfuku-ji|WD=Q1062452|lt=Sōfuku-ji (Fukuoka)|1=Sōfuku-ji}}, {{ill|Dazaifu|en|Dazaifu|WD=Q1179277|lt=Dazaifu|1=Dazaifu}}.
In {{ill|Ninji|en|Ninji|WD=Q1194262|lt=Ninji|1=Ninji}} 3 (1242), Sukeyoshi donated land to establish the {{ill|Jōten-ji|en|Jōten-ji|WD=Q3191242|lt=Shōten-ji|1=Jōten-ji}} in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata|1=Hakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11284628}}, fulfilling a promise made by his father, {{ill|Muto Sukeyori|ja|武藤資頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muto Sukeyori|1=Q11546530|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546530}}.<ref>渡辺文吉『武藤少弐興亡史』 海鳥社、1989th year (p.12)</ref>
Near the ruins of {{ill|Anyo-ji|ja|安養寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anyo-ji|1=Q11451679|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451679}}, one of the {{ill|Kannon-ji Temple|ja|観世音寺四十九子院跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon-ji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Dazaifu, Fukuoka|en|Dazaifu, Fukuoka|WD=Q728218|lt=Dazaifu|1=Dazaifu, Fukuoka}}, the burial site of Sukeyoshi is marked by a {{ill|Hōkyōintō|en|Hōkyōintō|WD=Q3146365|lt=Hōkyōintō|1=Hōkyōintō}} memorial alongside a five-tiered pagoda for Sukeyori.<ref>[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu26.html 九州国立博物館「西都 大宰府」記述あり。]</ref>
